(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=22810%2E878179%2E3zhlC_). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=22810%2E878179%2E3zhlC_).**

subject: This is why you can NEVER give up!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Defense Trust
body:
Failure to follow required verification procedures...
| **_FROM THE DESK OF RUDY W. GIULIANI_**
---
Dear Friend,
---
When I was arrested in Fulton County in the most violent jail in America, I could’ve made all the legal battles magically disappear overnight...
---
_...I could’ve betrayed the truth by going on camera, dropping to my knees, and saying that the 2020 election was perfectly fair, transparent, and completely free of fraud!_
---
In fact, some who were arrested __did__ take plea deals.
---
**But the President and I did NOT.**
---
Our position was clear: **__NEVER_ _SURRENDER_!_**
---
As the old saying goes, “I’d rather die on my feet than live on my knees.”
---
_And now, the TRUTH is finally coming out:_
---
**“Fulton County admits to verifying 315,000 votes in 2020 without poll worker signatures”**
_-Atlanta News First_
---
But unfortunately, bloodthirsty left-wing tyrants do not care about the truth.
---
**I am _still_ being PROSECUTED by a vengeful Democrat Attorney General who wants to imprison me as part of her re-election campaign platform _this_ year.**
---
And with reports of a blue wave coming, she thinks she can get away with it – because voters will not stop her.
---
_Well, I beg to differ..._
---
_The people can stop her right here, right now by signing onto the People’s Defense of Rudy W. Giuliani in light of new evidence that adds even MORE doubt to the integrity of the 2020 election._

GIULIANI Dear Friend, When I was arrested in Fulton County in the most violent jail in America, I could’ve made all the legal battles magically disappear overnight... ...I could’ve betrayed the truth by going on camera, dropping to my knees, and saying that the 2020 election was perfectly fair, transparent, and completely free of fraud! In fact, some who were arrested did take plea deals. But the President and I did NOT. Our position was clear: NEVER SURRENDER! As the old saying goes, “I’d rather die on my feet than live on my knees.” And now, the TRUTH is finally coming out: “Fulton County admits to verifying 315,000 votes in 2020 without poll worker signatures” -Atlanta News First But unfortunately, bloodthirsty left-wing tyrants do not care about the truth. I am still being PROSECUTED by a vengeful Democrat Attorney General who wants to imprison me as part of her re-election campaign platform this year. And with reports of a blue wave coming, she thinks she can get away with it – because voters will not stop her. Well, I beg to differ... name: Team Taylor
subject: This is no coincidence
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Taylor for Justice
body:
The only way we fight back against these forces is with grassroots support from people like you.
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20260201-1071&t=1&akid=1081%2E294397%2ErqNBAA)
Folks,
Former GOP Gov. Scott Walker is meddling in our Wisconsin Supreme Court race.
He’s actively helping our extreme opponent fundraise — opening the door to a national network of right-wing special interests who want to reshape the Wisconsin Supreme Court.
And he’s doing it right as we approach a critical public fundraising deadline tomorrow. **That’s no coincidence.**
Scott Walker and his allies have spent years trying to tilt our courts in their favor. Now, they see our race as their next opportunity — and they’re betting we won’t be ready.
**The only way we fight back against these forces is with grassroots support from people like you.**
**[Please, chip in $10 before tomorrow night’s deadline. Your contribution helps us stay ahead of whatever they’re plotting.](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20260201_1_ak.JC3pXf&amount=10&refcode=em20260201-1071&t=3&akid=1081%2E294397%2ErqNBAA)**

name: Virginians For Fair Elections
subject: Call to Activism in Virginia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Virginians for Fair Elections
body:
Eyes around the country are on Virginia’s election this spring.
# [](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/131?refcode=em20260201-108&t=1&akid=108%2E12592%2EAWnl04)
Hey,
In a short video, political commentator Joe Gallina outlines what’s at risk for the Commonwealth’s congressional districts — and _why_ the amendment being put to a vote in Virginia this spring is so important.
[**Click here for the full video:**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/341?t=2&akid=108%2E12592%2EAWnl04)
[****](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/341?t=3&akid=108%2E12592%2EAWnl04)
To quote Joe: “This amendment gives Virginians the **final say** on congressional maps, **_not politicians behind closed doors._** ”
At a moment when Virginians’ voices are under threat, this amendment _protects_ them in Washington. It levels the playing field by allowing voters to review proposed maps before anything happens, ensuring that their elections are fair, transparent, and _truly_ representative of their will.
This message is resonating with people everywhere, and all eyes are on Virginia to see what happens next.
**We’re ready to turn this momentum into results.** We need to make sure the people of Virginia understand what this amendment means for them, and how a YES vote will protect fair representation in this year’s midterm elections before it’s too late.
[**Can you chip in to help keep our momentum going? Every dollar goes towards spreading our message and ensuring Virginians know the high stakes of this election before casting their vote.**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260201_1.Yjfwp6&amount=3&refcode=20260201-108&t=4&akid=108%2E12592%2EAWnl04)
**[Donate $3 ››](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260201_1.Yjfwp6&amount=3&refcode=20260201-108&t=23&akid=108%2E12592%2EAWnl04)**
Thank you for standing with us,
Virginians for Fair Elections

Trump just launched an AI "Genesis Mission"--because apparently the cloud now needs a government life jacket.

# ['In the beginning...there was AI? Trump's Genesis Mission: A Bold Leap for AI, But a Call for Government Lifelines in an Unprofitable Frontier" by Steve Abramowicz ](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6729589&post_id=186506663&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NTA2NjYzLCJpYXQiOjE3Njk5NjAxMjcsImV4cCI6MTc3MjU1MjEyNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTY3Mjk1ODkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.2Z_gx9hQSA5B4wmPgJpAKTrea6DJsJnADtKipekJxbs)
### Trump just launched an AI "Genesis Mission"--because apparently the cloud now needs a government life jacket.
| | [Heartland Journal ®](https://substack.com/@heartlandjournal)
---
| Feb 1
---
| [](https://substack.com/@heartlandjournal)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6729589&post_id=186506663&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NTA2NjYzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2OTk2MDEyNywiZXhwIjoxNzcyNTUyMTI3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNjcyOTU4OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TB7du16GAC97hXiAEbaZ3wkwfPrIiDaD9lcgGlETptI&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

---

| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/3db3162d-35b6-40c7-b16e-85ccbbaa2137?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In a move evoking the high-stakes urgency of World War II's atomic race, President Donald Trump has signed an executive order launching the Genesis Mission, a sweeping federal initiative to propel American artificial intelligence into the stratosphere of scientific and national security dominance.

Announced on a crisp Monday afternoon in late 2025, the order positions the U.S. government as the ultimate venture capitalist for AI, channeling vast federal datasets, computational firepower, and public-private synergies to tackle challenges from biotechnology to nuclear fusion. Dubbed "comparable in urgency and ambition to the Manhattan Project," the Genesis Mission isn't just policy theater--it's a recognition that AI's promise demands resources beyond what profit-driven enterprises can sustainably muster alone.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9oZWFydGxhbmRqb3VybmFsLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.hInWEv4zza6LKg8BLCX07VFUeAXsAszovvsQrK90P3M?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=186506663)
Yet, as this landmark effort unfolds, it underscores a harsh reality: AI's business model remains a black hole for private capital, riddled with astronomical costs, protracted timelines, and diffuse returns. To secure America's edge, the Genesis Mission must evolve into a blueprint for strategic bailouts--government infusions that rescue and redirect an industry teetering on the brink of unprofitability.

At its core, the executive order establishes the Genesis Mission as a national crusade to "supercharge American AI research, development, and scientific applications."

Tasked with execution is Michael Kratsios, Trump's Assistant for Science and Technology and director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy, who will orchestrate the integration of siloed agency data and infrastructure. The Secretary of Energy, Chris Wright, faces a tight 90-day deadline to catalog available systems and datasets, drawing in resources from industry heavyweights like AMD, Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), and Nvidia. These partnerships aren't mere cameos; they're foundational, building on recent Department of Energy announcements for next-generation supercomputers at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, equipped with Nvidia's high-powered chips to fuel AI model training.

The mission's ambitions are audacious. It aims to harness the "world's largest collection" of federal scientific datasets--decades of taxpayer-funded treasures in fields like advanced manufacturing, robotics, biotech, and energy--to birth "scientific foundation models" and AI agents capable of hypothesizing, automating workflows, and accelerating breakthroughs.
Within 270 days, the initiative pledges real-world applications, transforming abstract algorithms into tools for national priorities: bolstering energy dominance, fortifying workforce productivity, and amplifying returns on public R&D investments. This builds directly on the National Artificial Intelligence Research Resource (NAIRR), a 2020 pilot coalition of federal agencies (Defense, NASA, NIH) and tech titans (OpenAI, Google, Palantir), now supercharged under Trump's July 2025 AI Action Plan.

Experts hail the move as a "strong signal" to global rivals, critics call is a ploy to make tech 'too big to fail'. Keegan McBride, a senior policy advisor at the Tony Blair Institute, notes that "AI has the potential to transform the entire scientific, research, and discovery pipeline."

Lynne Parker, former deputy chief technology officer under Biden and now an AI policy veteran, emphasizes the public-interest imperative: "Government support for AI research builds the foundation for new breakthroughs and helps keep innovation aligned with the public interest."

Without it, she warns, the U.S. risks "ceding leadership in the technologies that will define our economy, our security, and our daily lives." Secretary Wright echoes this, celebrating "new and creative partnerships" that "prove America leads when private-public partners build together."

But beneath the fanfare lies a sobering subtext: private enterprise, for all its ingenuity, is ill-equipped to bankroll AI at this scale without a safety net. The business model of frontier AI development is fundamentally unprofitable in the short-to-medium term, a fact the Genesis Mission implicitly acknowledges by leaning so heavily on federal crutches. Consider the economics. Training a single state-of-the-art large language model like GPT-4 reportedly costs upwards of $100 million in compute alone, not counting the billions in R&D for underlying architectures. Data acquisition? Private firms scrape the web or license scraps, but true scientific datasets--curated, verified, and vast--reside in government vaults, untapped due to privacy, security, and antitrust hurdles. Compute infrastructure? Hyperscalers like AWS and Azure charge premium rates, yet even they grapple with energy demands that could power small cities, driving marginal costs skyward.

This isn't hyperbole; it's arithmetic. Venture capital in AI has ballooned to $50 billion annually, yet profitability eludes most players. OpenAI, despite its unicorn status, operates at a $5 billion annual loss, subsidized by Microsoft infusions that border on philanthropy. Anthropic and xAI burn cash on talent wars and GPU hoarding, with ROI timelines stretching decades. Why? AI's value accrues as a public good--diffuse, non-rivalrous, and prone to free-riding. Innovations like AlphaFold revolutionized protein folding for all, but the originating firm reaps scant direct revenue. National security applications, from predictive cyber defenses to autonomous drones, demand classified data inaccessible to pure-play privates. And the risks? Model hallucinations, ethical blowback, regulatory whiplash--these amplify failure probabilities, deterring investors who crave 10x returns in five years, not moonshots in 50.

Enter the case for bailouts: targeted government rescues to sustain AI's private pioneers until societal dividends kick in. The Genesis Mission is a de facto bailout framework, centralizing resources via the "American Science and Security Platform" to de-risk private involvement. But it must go further--explicit fiscal commitments, not just datasets and deadlines. History validates this. The Manhattan Project, invoked here, devoured $2 billion (1940s dollars) in public funds, birthing the atomic age without a single profitable prototype. Apollo's $25 billion moonshot spawned semiconductors and GPS, economic engines worth trillions, yet no private firm could have shouldered it amid Cold War imperatives. ARPANET, DARPA's internet progenitor, was a government gamble that privatized into a $10 trillion behemoth. These weren't subsidies; they were strategic bailouts for technologies too vital, too costly, and too uncertain for market forces alone.

In AI's case, the stakes are existential. China, unburdened by quarterly earnings, pours $15 billion yearly into state-backed AI, outpacing U.S. privates in patents and compute clusters. Without bailouts, American firms risk offshoring talent or folding under cost pressures--witness the startup graveyard of 2024, where 40% of AI ventures shuttered pre-revenue. Bailouts could take forms: direct grants for NAIRR expansions, tax credits for federal dataset integrations, or low-interest loans for Oak Ridge-scale builds. Frame them as "national security multipliers," not corporate welfare: every dollar in AI R&D yields $7 in GDP growth, per McKinsey estimates. The Genesis Mission's 270-day sprint to applications? That's bailout urgency--fast-tracking prototypes to validate models, attract follow-on private capital, and deter foreign poaching.

Critics might balk at "picking winners," but the alternative is atrophy. Private AI thrives on government scaffolding: the CHIPS Act's $52 billion already props chip fabs; extend that logic to software and data. Parker's admonition rings true: decades of federal research underpin today's iPhones and mRNA vaccines, yet we "seldom consider" the upstream investments.
Bailouts ensure reciprocity--privates innovate atop public foundations, then repay via licensing, jobs, and breakthroughs.

David Sacks, special adviser to the White House for AI and crypto, has weighed in on the AI "doomer" debate. In a widely discussed post on [X](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/18e942a7-7a85-47df-a71d-c3fd96272177?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Sacks laid out a vision for AI's present and future, arguing the fearmongering in recent years about AI's malevolence is deeply misguided. Sacks added that job loss fears are overhyped, and instead, people stand to benefit most by learning to harness AI for new opportunities.

Economists at the Federal Reserve have been studying the same question, and their "modal forecast" is for a significant boost to labor productivity. Still, they warn it may not evolve into a general-purpose technology such as electricity or the internet. They ask, what if, it temporarily raises productivity growth before fading amid widespread adoption? That would render it an invention more like the light bulb.

As Trump's order ripples outward, the Genesis Mission stands as a clarion call: AI isn't a Silicon Valley sideshow; it's a Manhattan-scale imperative demanding public resolve. By embracing bailouts, the U.S. doesn't just report progress--it authors the future. Let private enterprise chase efficiencies; let government bailout the bold, ensuring AI's flame illuminates American horizons, not eclipses them. In this race, hesitation is forfeiture. The order is signed; now, let private capital fund the mission.

_Editorial comments expressed in this column are the sole opinion of the writer._

clean_body: Trump just launched an AI "Genesis Mission"--because apparently the cloud now needs a government life jacket. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Trump just launched an AI "Genesis Mission"--because apparently the cloud now needs a government life jacket. | | | Feb 1 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- In a move evoking the high-stakes urgency of World War II's atomic race, President Donald Trump has signed an executive order launching the Genesis Mission, a sweeping federal initiative to propel American artificial intelligence into the stratosphere of scientific and national security dominance. Announced on a crisp Monday afternoon in late 2025, the order positions the U.S. government as the ultimate venture capitalist for AI, channeling vast federal datasets, computational firepower, and public-private synergies to tackle challenges from biotechnology to nuclear fusion. Dubbed "comparable in urgency and ambition to the Manhattan Project," the Genesis Mission isn't just policy theater--it's a recognition that AI's promise demands resources beyond what profit-driven enterprises can sustainably muster alone. Yet, as this landmark effort unfolds, it underscores a harsh reality: AI's business model remains a black hole for private capital, riddled with astronomical costs, protracted timelines, and diffuse returns. To secure America's edge, the Genesis Mission must evolve into a blueprint for strategic bailouts--government infusions that rescue and redirect an industry teetering on the brink of unprofitability. At its core, the executive order establishes the Genesis Mission as a national crusade to "supercharge American AI research, development, and scientific applications." Tasked with execution is Michael Kratsios, Trump's Assistant for Science and Technology and director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy, who will orchestrate the integration of siloed agency data and infrastructure. The Secretary of Energy, Chris Wright, faces a tight 90-day deadline to catalog available systems and datasets, drawing in resources from industry heavyweights like AMD, Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), and Nvidia. These partnerships aren't mere cameos; they're foundational, building on recent Department of Energy announcements for next-generation supercomputers at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, equipped with Nvidia's high-powered chips to fuel AI model training. The mission's ambitions are audacious. It aims to harness the "world's largest collection" of federal scientific datasets--decades of taxpayer-funded treasures in fields like advanced manufacturing, robotics, biotech, and energy--to birth "scientific foundation models" and AI agents capable of hypothesizing, automating workflows, and accelerating breakthroughs. Within 270 days, the initiative pledges real-world applications, transforming abstract algorithms into tools for national priorities: bolstering energy dominance, fortifying workforce productivity, and amplifying returns on public R&D investments. This builds directly on the National Artificial Intelligence Research Resource (NAIRR), a 2020 pilot coalition of federal agencies (Defense, NASA, NIH) and tech titans (OpenAI, Google, Palantir), now supercharged under Trump's July 2025 AI Action Plan. Experts hail the move as a "strong signal" to global rivals, critics call is a ploy to make tech 'too big to fail'. Keegan McBride, a senior policy advisor at the Tony Blair Institute, notes that "AI has the potential to transform the entire scientific, research, and discovery pipeline." Lynne Parker, former deputy chief technology officer under Biden and now an AI policy veteran, emphasizes the public-interest imperative: "Government support for AI research builds the foundation for new breakthroughs and helps keep innovation aligned with the public interest." Without it, she warns, the U.S. risks "ceding leadership in the technologies that will define our economy, our security, and our daily lives." Secretary Wright echoes this, celebrating "new and creative partnerships" that "prove America leads when private-public partners build together." But beneath the fanfare lies a sobering subtext: private enterprise, for all its ingenuity, is ill-equipped to bankroll AI at this scale without a safety net. The business model of frontier AI development is fundamentally unprofitable in the short-to-medium term, a fact the Genesis Mission implicitly acknowledges by leaning so heavily on federal crutches. Consider the economics. Training a single state-of-the-art large language model like GPT-4 reportedly costs upwards of $100 million in compute alone, not counting the billions in R&D for underlying architectures. Data acquisition? Private firms scrape the web or license scraps, but true scientific datasets--curated, verified, and vast--reside in government vaults, untapped due to privacy, security, and antitrust hurdles. Compute infrastructure? Hyperscalers like AWS and Azure charge premium rates, yet even they grapple with energy demands that could power small cities, driving marginal costs skyward. This isn't hyperbole; it's arithmetic. Venture capital in AI has ballooned to $50 billion annually, yet profitability eludes most players. OpenAI, despite its unicorn status, operates at a $5 billion annual loss, subsidized by Microsoft infusions that border on philanthropy. Anthropic and xAI burn cash on talent wars and GPU hoarding, with ROI timelines stretching decades. Why? AI's value accrues as a public good--diffuse, non-rivalrous, and prone to free-riding. Innovations like AlphaFold revolutionized protein folding for all, but the originating firm reaps scant direct revenue. National security applications, from predictive cyber defenses to autonomous drones, demand classified data inaccessible to pure-play privates. And the risks? Model hallucinations, ethical blowback, regulatory whiplash--these amplify failure probabilities, deterring investors who crave 10x returns in five years, not moonshots in 50. Enter the case for bailouts: targeted government rescues to sustain AI's private pioneers until societal dividends kick in. The Genesis Mission is a de facto bailout framework, centralizing resources via the "American Science and Security Platform" to de-risk private involvement. But it must go further--explicit fiscal commitments, not just datasets and deadlines. History validates this. The Manhattan Project, invoked here, devoured $2 billion (1940s dollars) in public funds, birthing the atomic age without a single profitable prototype. Apollo's $25 billion moonshot spawned semiconductors and GPS, economic engines worth trillions, yet no private firm could have shouldered it amid Cold War imperatives. ARPANET, DARPA's internet progenitor, was a government gamble that privatized into a $10 trillion behemoth. These weren't subsidies; they were strategic bailouts for technologies too vital, too costly, and too uncertain for market forces alone. In AI's case, the stakes are existential. China, unburdened by quarterly earnings, pours $15 billion yearly into state-backed AI, outpacing U.S. privates in patents and compute clusters. Without bailouts, American firms risk offshoring talent or folding under cost pressures--witness the startup graveyard of 2024, where 40% of AI ventures shuttered pre-revenue. Bailouts could take forms: direct grants for NAIRR expansions, tax credits for federal dataset integrations, or low-interest loans for Oak Ridge-scale builds. Frame them as "national security multipliers," not corporate welfare: every dollar in AI R&D yields $7 in GDP growth, per McKinsey estimates. The Genesis Mission's 270-day sprint to applications? That's bailout urgency--fast-tracking prototypes to validate models, attract follow-on private capital, and deter foreign poaching. Critics might balk at "picking winners," but the alternative is atrophy. Private AI thrives on government scaffolding: the CHIPS Act's $52 billion already props chip fabs; extend that logic to software and data. Parker's admonition rings true: decades of federal research underpin today's iPhones and mRNA vaccines, yet we "seldom consider" the upstream investments. Bailouts ensure reciprocity--privates innovate atop public foundations, then repay via licensing, jobs, and breakthroughs. David Sacks, special adviser to the White House for AI and crypto, has weighed in on the AI "doomer" debate. In a widely discussed post on , Sacks laid out a vision for AI's present and future, arguing the fearmongering in recent years about AI's malevolence is deeply misguided. Sacks added that job loss fears are overhyped, and instead, people stand to benefit most by learning to harness AI for new opportunities. Economists at the Federal Reserve have been studying the same question, and their "modal forecast" is for a significant boost to labor productivity. Still, they warn it may not evolve into a general-purpose technology such as electricity or the internet. They ask, what if, it temporarily raises productivity growth before fading amid widespread adoption? That would render it an invention more like the light bulb. As Trump's order ripples outward, the Genesis Mission stands as a clarion call: AI isn't a Silicon Valley sideshow; it's a Manhattan-scale imperative demanding public resolve. By embracing bailouts, the U.S. doesn't just report progress--it authors the future. Let private enterprise chase efficiencies; let government bailout the bold, ensuring AI's flame illuminates American horizons, not eclipses them. In this race, hesitation is forfeiture. The order is signed; now, let private capital fund the mission. Editorial comments expressed in this column are the sole opinion of the writer. This Substack is reader-supported. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. If you like what you read, please forward it to a friend. We only grow with your help, and thanks for doing it! subject: Expose the Billionaires Profiting from ICE – Help Spread Our Video Now

body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34987?t=1011&refcode=email-20260201_v&akid=10155%2E5885482%2EKdYIz0 ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis,

If we’re going to stop ICE from terrorizing our communities, we have to
follow the money – straight to the police state oligarchs cashing in on
cruelty.

While we mobilize in the streets to get ICE out, some of the richest and
most powerful players in America are making a fortune from pain and fear.
Last year, GOP lawmakers greenlit a staggering $170 BILLION for DHS and
ICE – much of which goes straight into the pockets of oligarchs with big
new ICE contracts. These billionaires see families as dollar signs, not
human beings.

One thing is clear: if we’re going to stop ICE, we need to stop the
billionaires and corporations turning mass deportation and violence into
big business. The problem? The mainstream media barely whispers their
names.

That’s why Our Revolution just released a hard-hitting new video exposing
the billionaires behind ICE’s horrors. It shows exactly how these police
state profiteers are fueling policies that tear our communities apart.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34987?t=1057&amount=22&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&refcode=email-20260201nd&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10155%2E5885482%2EKdYIz0 ]Will you donate now to help us flood the internet with this bold video
exposé – and make sure every American knows who’s bankrolling ICE?

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation to Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34987?t=1042&express_lane=true&amount=9&refcode=email-20260201nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10155%2E5885482%2EKdYIz0 ]CHIP IN $9 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34987?t=1043&express_lane=true&amount=22&refcode=email-20260201nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10155%2E5885482%2EKdYIz0 ]CHIP IN $22
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34987?t=1045&express_lane=true&amount=27&refcode=email-20260201nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10155%2E5885482%2EKdYIz0 ]CHIP IN $27 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34987?t=1046&express_lane=true&amount=46&refcode=email-20260201nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10155%2E5885482%2EKdYIz0 ]CHIP IN $46
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34987?t=1047&express_lane=true&amount=66&refcode=email-20260201nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10155%2E5885482%2EKdYIz0 ]CHIP IN $66 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34987?t=1048&amount=9&refcode=email-20260201nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10155%2E5885482%2EKdYIz0 ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT

The truth is billionaires are profiting every time ICE rips a child from
their parents. In 2025 alone, ICE spent $1.2 BILLION on private prison
contractors, with $500 million going to GEO Group and $200 million to
CoreCivic. ICE spent $500 million of taxpayer money on private
transportation including air charter services to companies like CSI
Aviation.

The police state billionaires KNEW that electing Trump would mean massive
profits. So they pumped over $4 million into his election coffers. The
result? Exponential growth for private ICE jails, record contracts, and
corporate boardrooms celebrating the misery of our neighbors. Since then,
they’ve pocketed $5.2 BILLION in taxpayer handouts, an 11,050% ROI!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34988?source=email-20260201&t=1060&akid=10155%2E5885482%2EKdYIz0 ]ICE oligarchs graphic showing their profit

These oligarchs are betting that Americans won’t connect the dots. But our
movement’s power comes from making these ugly truths undeniable. The more
people who see our video, the more people who will demand an end to this
profiteering and the ICE brutality it fuels.

When we expose the oligarchs profiting from abuse, we build the pressure
to divest, restrict contracts, and shut down their business model of pain.
This is why we’re quickly raising the resources for targeted ads in
battleground states, so every activist, voter, and ally sees this exposé.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34987?t=1054&amount=22&refcode=email-20260201nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=10155%2E5885482%2EKdYIz0 ]Let’s show the public who’s cashing in – and make it impossible for them
to hide. Will you fuel this push and help us turn up the pressure on the
billionaires behind the ICE terror machine?

We’ve taken on Wall Street, Big Oil, and Big Pharma before – now it’s time
to shine the spotlight on the profiteers hiding behind ICE.

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

name: Ro Khanna
subject: Why I’m supporting Zach Shrewsbury
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]
Dear Peter,
Let’s be frank – Democrats must win back the majority in the US Senate in 2026. That means we need candidates who can inspire real people, take on entrenched power, and build campaigns rooted in working-class values.
That’s why I’m proud to endorse Zach Shrewsbury for the United States Senate in West Virginia.
Before I tell you more, will you consider making a split contribution to help launch Zach’s campaign today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rk-zach-shrewsbury-split?refcode=20260131]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rk-zach-shrewsbury-split?refcode=20260131]
Zach is a Marine Corps veteran, the grandson of a coal miner, a working-class Democrat, and a lifelong West Virginian. He knows the hollers and backroads of his state, not just the inside of a lobbyist’s office.
While Washington insiders have grown comfortable with the status quo, families in West Virginia have paid the price: shuttered hospitals, lost jobs, deepening addiction, and a political system that too often answers to corporate donors instead of communities.
Zach is running to change that.
He’s organizing a campaign rooted in people power. But that only works when supporters step up early and show that they believe in leadership guided by values, not big money or political dynasties.
If you believe West Virginia deserves a Senator who fights for economic justice, healthcare, and working-class dignity, I hope you’ll join me in supporting Zach’s campaign today.
👉 Contribute now to support Zach Shrewsbury and my work in Congress. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rk-zach-shrewsbury-split?refcode=20260131]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rk-zach-shrewsbury-split?refcode=20260131]
Thank you for your activism and everything that you do.
In solidarity,
Ro Khanna
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rk-zach-shrewsbury-split?refcode=20260131]

name: President Trump
subject: I’m drinking a Trump diet soda!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Never Surrender, Inc
body:
And all I could think about was... YOU!
| | | | I was thousands of miles in the air. Drinking a Trump diet soda. And all I could think about was...

---
****
# YOU!

It’s Trump here. And we’ve just run into a problem...

[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
**_YOU were the one that stood by my side through all the witch hunts._****__**
**__**
**_YOU played a critical role in my massive landslide win in November 2024._****__**
**__**
**_YOU joined my team of 2025 Trump Cabinet Members and your advice helped me get through some very sticky situations._** **__****__**
But the problem is:

YOU haven’t renewed your annual membership and it’s now February.
I know you would never give up on me or the MAGA movement but I’m getting concerned.
**[Please let me know if you’re okay? AND that you’re STILL on my side by reactivating your Trump Cabinet Membership before I step foot in the Oval Office.>](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)**
Thank you,
President Donald J Trump

name: JD Vance
subject: I promised some important people a response from Peter.

name: Team DNC
subject: This is not a fundraising email:
body:
---
Derek, We know that you get bombarded with emails at the end of the month as _everyone_ is facing fundraising deadlines and trying to hit their goals. So **thank you** for sticking with us through that and for opening this email. It’s supporters like you who make our work possible. In times like these, it’s easy to feel overwhelmed by everything — the news, the stakes of this next election, the rising cost of living. Thank you for continuing to dedicate your time and energy to causes that matter, even when things feel dire. **Derek, today we’re not asking for money.** We just want to say thank you, and let you know that if you’d like to receive more frequent updates on what Democrats and the DNC are doing to fight back and make sure that we win in 2026, [**we invite you to subscribe to our official newsletter, The Blue Print.**](https://blueprint.democrats.org/?refcode=em_20260201_5968_dc&amount=7&akid=5968%2E22930333%2EJBI2Ci) Subscribers to The Blue Print get weekly updates from DNC leaders and staff on the work Democrats are doing on the ground to take back the House and Senate and resist the Trump administration’s agenda. **[SUBSCRIBE TO THE BLUE PRINT](https://blueprint.democrats.org/?refcode=em_20260201_5968_dc&akid=5968%2E22930333%2EJBI2Ci)** Thank you for your support, The Democrats P.S. If you felt like you got too many emails from us at the end of the month, and would like us to slow down the pace, you can click [**here**](https://ak.democrats.org/signup/receive-less-email/?refcode=em_20260201_5968_dc&akid=5968%2E22930333%2EJBI2Ci) to receive fewer emails. No hard feelings!

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

name: Dillan Horton
subject: Eric Jones for Congress - Office Opening Party
body:
Good morning folks,

Here is one last reminder about the opening of our campaign office in downtown Davis. It's later today (Feb 1st) from 11am to 2pm, and the address is 503 4th Street, Suite B in Davis. It'll be a great time to meet Eric & the team if you haven't already, and also a great chance to get connected with folks similarly motivated to see more proactive representation in Congress. If you're available, we hope to see you there.

Best of wishes,

Dillan Horton
dillan@ericjones.us ~ (214)604-9953
Field Director ~ Eric Jones for Congress

subject: this can’t be right…correct the record (Trump Approval Poll)
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23881%2E4449941%2Euj7zoR) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23881%2E4449941%2Euj7zoR) [ **🔴 DPWILLIS67@GMAIL.COM'S IMMEDIATE RESPONSE REQUIRED 🔴**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/72932?t=3&akid=23881%2E4449941%2Euj7zoR) Team, we’re looking back at Trump’s disastrous first year of his second term – _and asking HOW SO MANY Americans STILL approve of Donald Trump?!_

After officially reaching one-year back in office, [**we’re conducting an URGENT POLL to understand his approval rating in your state. name: Joe Jaworski
subject: HUGE win in Texas
disclaimer_text: paid for by Joe Jaworski Campaign
body:
Last night’s special election in Texas delivered a serious blow to MAGA Republicans. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/jaworskifortexas.com/last-night-14192497?e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | [ ](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=f6955441fa&e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | , **ABSOLUTELY HUGE.** Last night’s special election in Texas delivered a serious blow to MAGA Republicans, and it showed something they hate admitting: **when money collides with organization and enthusiasm, money loses.** As it should. Democrats did the work. Voters responded. Why? Because Democrats understand what voters actually want, and we’re willing to show up and fight for it. In a district Republicans have held since the early 1990s, voters flipped a Texas Senate seat from Republican to Democratic. **Democrat Taylor Rehmet, an Air Force veteran and machinist, defeated a right-wing Republican backed by Greg Abbott and Donald Trump.** Republicans tried to buy this race. In the final days alone, they spent hundreds of thousands of dollars while Rehmet spent nothing. Over the course of the campaign, Republicans spent millions more. And they still lost. Even more remarkable: this was a district Trump won by 17 points in 2024. Democrats just won it by more than 14 points. That’s a massive swing, and it happened right here in Texas. This result makes one thing clear. MAGA has no mandate for the unconstitutional death and destruction they’re pushing. When voters are given a real choice, they reject it. So let me say it plainly: **GET OUT OF THE WAY, MAGA.** This is what happens when Democrats organize, stay disciplined, and trust voters. And it’s exactly how we’re going to win in 2026. [**Will you help us achieve the same victory by contributing to our campaign**](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=1041f52b5a&e=5d8d80652b) or [**signing up to volunteer for our final push into the March Primary?**](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=8a0c05705d&e=5d8d80652b) Onward!

clean_body: Last night’s special election in Texas delivered a serious blow to MAGA Republicans. | | | | | | | , ABSOLUTELY HUGE. Last night’s special election in Texas delivered a serious blow to MAGA Republicans, and it showed something they hate admitting: when money collides with organization and enthusiasm, money loses. As it should. Democrats did the work. Voters responded. Why? Because Democrats understand what voters actually want, and we’re willing to show up and fight for it. In a district Republicans have held since the early 1990s, voters flipped a Texas Senate seat from Republican to Democratic. Democrat Taylor Rehmet, an Air Force veteran and machinist, defeated a right-wing Republican backed by Greg Abbott and Donald Trump. Republicans tried to buy this race. In the final days alone, they spent hundreds of thousands of dollars while Rehmet spent nothing. Over the course of the campaign, Republicans spent millions more. And they still lost. Even more remarkable: this was a district Trump won by 17 points in 2024. Democrats just won it by more than 14 points. That’s a massive swing, and it happened right here in Texas. This result makes one thing clear. MAGA has no mandate for the unconstitutional death and destruction they’re pushing. When voters are given a real choice, they reject it. So let me say it plainly: GET OUT OF THE WAY, MAGA. This is what happens when Democrats organize, stay disciplined, and trust voters. The deadline to register to vote in the March primaries is tomorrow. If we don’t act, thousands of voices could be left out of this important election.
**That is exactly why Powered by People exists: to make sure everyone has a chance to be heard.**
We train volunteers, we register people, we show up for voters when the system intentionally makes it hard. And in these final hours, every second matters.
But we can’t do it alone. To reach, inform, and register voters and turn them out up until _the last minute_ , we need resources—staff, volunteers, tools, and fuel for the work on the ground.
It’s the support of this grassroots team that makes it all possible, keeping us reaching voters who might otherwise be left behind.
This isn’t just about numbers. It’s about people—neighbors, friends, coworkers, and families who deserve a say in the elections that shape their lives. And we need to make sure their voices are heard.

### What a college trip to Los Angeles revealed about who America protects--and who it's willing to sacrifice.
| | [Dana DuBois](https://substack.com/@danadubois) and [Blue Amp Media](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---
| Feb 1
---
| [](https://substack.com/@danadubois)| | [](https://substack.com/@blueamp)

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=185929429&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1OTI5NDI5LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2OTk2NTExOSwiZXhwIjoxNzcyNTU3MTE5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzcyMjUzMiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.rywEXCuVoHUwpId7OMY7xAT4TiYK-YTmde3LamBegAA&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/blueamp/p/i-took-my-daughter-shopping-america?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9cde9e44-e583-449d-bdde-2934231ee0e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Who does America protect? Rodeo Drive and a tear-gassed protest tell very different stories about safety and power.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/56afce69-62d1-4c7e-a42f-7d4afa05e0dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Blue Amp Media is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.TIXAykHLM9VWAgO2z9ftMl5H1yknY8h9Nd3jRiVd0U4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=185929429)
* * *
"Well, we do have college to pay for, but we also need to live a little, right?" I say, smiling at Adom in response to the price he's just revealed for the adorable Prada evening bag my daughter has looped over her forearm.
$2,600.
For a _handbag_.
I catch the flash of disbelief on my daughter's face, even as I keep mine -- if not beatific, then at least placid.
It isn't hard to smile. Adom is an adorable 30-something salesman who loves his job and treats us with nothing but kindness and good humor-- showering my eighteen-year-old with handbags to try on and gushing about what's great about each one. She's delighted.
"Absolutely! You've gotta live it up!" he says.
He means it, and most days, I do too. But today, my capacity for joy is blunted by the news I read just hours earlier.
Across the country, another 30-something man didn't get to "live it up" -- or even to live. He was in Minneapolis during a federal immigration enforcement operation, trying to help someone nearby, and he didn't come home.
The man was Alex Pretti, a 37-year-old ICU nurse at the Veterans Affairs hospital who cared deeply for the most injured and vulnerable patients. Witnesses and video footage suggest he was filming and trying to assist others when federal agents opened fire. The circumstances of his death have sparked widespread protests and outrage.
I'm angry. I'm scared. My heart feels heavy. I'm supposed to be present for this precious time with my daughter. I'm also supposed to teach her, to lead by example.
"Mom, put your phone down and live in the moment," she says.
It's good advice. Is it good advice _today_?
We walk on down Rodeo Drive. Every store is flawless, guarded, designed -- surreal in its retail grandeur.
I sigh as we continue our walk, observing the handsome men in immaculate designer suits standing beside velvet ropes in front of every store. They aren't there to sell handbags. They're there to decide who gets inside.
There's more visible security protecting leather goods than I've seen protecting people in our cities.
Who do we show up for, I wonder. In the United States right now, the answer feels clear: we protect products over people.
We no longer reliably honor our inalienable rights unless they come with a velvet rope and a logo.
It's fun to window-shop on Rodeo, to marvel at all the beautiful things. But I feel the contrast tightening in my gut.
Luxury is safe. A man is dead.
And I keep circling the same question: who -- or what -- does America protect now?
* * *
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.TIXAykHLM9VWAgO2z9ftMl5H1yknY8h9Nd3jRiVd0U4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=185929429)
* * *
My daughter and I are in Los Angeles for yet another college tour.
This school has offered her a generous scholarship, including a travel budget to come see the campus. So here we are. On the flight down, she announces, "I know I'm going to love LA." But she's lukewarm on the school itself, to my surprise.
"Give it a chance," I tell her. "Tour it with an open mind." It's a good school, and the idea of her living on the West Coast, in a blue state, feels like a comfort to me.
Or does it?
The siege in Minneapolis has been going on for more than a week. I've never been there, but I have a close friend from Seattle who is, and know several others who have moved from Seattle to Minneapolis. I've long thought of us as sister cities -- big blue dots, liberal politics, weather most people find unappealing but those of us who choose it love, and a shared GenX soundtrack of 90s bands and [losers](https://substack.com/redirect/743393b6-d5ce-4647-9634-3efc2b06de0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Even Cameron Crowe seemed to get it: the _Singles_ [soundtrack](https://substack.com/redirect/ca809f0a-31b1-4a01-a29c-82450961a590?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was comprised almost entirely of Seattle bands plus Paul Westerberg of the Replacements -- a Minneapolis icon. We're not the same, but we're kin.
I'm hyper-aware that where Minneapolis goes, Seattle may follow.
They're known for [hotdish](https://substack.com/redirect/d6c8e692-9782-4a74-af12-895a29e2c96d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) niceness; we're known for the Seattle Freeze, coffee, and heroin. Neither city is famous for fighting.
But those Minneapolis folks?
They're warriors. Heroes. I see it now.
They're out in subzero temperatures. They're literally whistleblowing to warn their neighbors. And in a city with deep Northern European roots, they're rising up to protect everyone -- especially their brown and Black neighbors.
Will Seattle do the same? I don't know. I hope so.
Could I put myself on the front lines and risk tear gas, arrest, or even death at the hands of the worst of what our nation can unleash: a scrambling gang of angry, poorly trained, overpaid, and over-empowered bullies who provoke, detain, and disappear people without just cause or consequence?
I'm not sure I have that flavor of bravery.
And could I live with my child alone in a faraway city and forced to do the same? Absolutely not.
But I know this much: I need to get clear -- fast -- about what I'm willing to do, and what I'm willing to risk.
They could be waiting for us when we get home.
And my child has a college decision looming.
* * *
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=185929429&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1OTI5NDI5LCJpYXQiOjE3Njk5NjUxMTksImV4cCI6MTc3MjU1NzExOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7bNzhj9l1wbwwf5XcKiGx3nOV6Sn3hrfJPC0PQQCl04)
* * *
I'm not surprised my daughter does, indeed, love LA.
She has always been brand- and beauty-conscious. It isn't who I am, but she isn't me, and that's okay. I don't see it as a moral failing when she wants to visit Erewhon to see the [$20 single strawberry](https://substack.com/redirect/4a5b783b-8b99-4142-bdaa-0a74b36b2f61?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (sold out) or drink the [$20 Hailey Bieber smoothie](https://substack.com/redirect/dd2df1e4-6324-41d8-a4fd-3a29e6fc3271?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (delicious, like most strawberry-banana smoothies). I see her as aligned with her Gen Z peers, and sometimes, I indulge these interests.
After a day wandering Santa Monica Pier and Venice Beach, she is ready for glamour.
"Mom, I want to see _fancy_ LA," she says.
I look up from the grim news out of Minneapolis. Of course she wants this. And of course, we have to keep moving. Spending the day buried in headlines won't bring Alex back or make the protests safer. My daughter still deserves her vacation day, and I'm determined to give it to her.
Of course, Rodeo Drive it is.
She's never seen _Pretty Woman_ , so I insist we watch a few iconic scenes in our hotel room first. I watch an impossibly young Julia Roberts clomp down Rodeo in her iconic white-and-blue hooker outfit. It was a star-making role. My daughter is awestruck.
"She's so beautiful!"
She's only known Julia as the mom in _Wonder_ , not as the devastating wild-haired ingenue she once was.
In the clip, Julia's character enters a Rodeo Drive shop asking for "something conservative" and is dismissed with a prim, "I don't think we have anything for you. You're obviously in the wrong place." The shop clerks are stuffy, well-coiffed, tightly buttoned -- more like Southern matrons than the style icons my daughter expects.
Our YouTube clip skips ahead to the shopping montage, set to Roy Orbison's "Pretty Woman."
"Those clothes are super ugly," my daughter opines. She's not wrong. They're of their era, but it's a conservative slice of it -- prudish, matronly, nothing that appeals to a modern teen or, frankly, to me.
"But wait for it," I tell her.
The line is coming -- _you know the line _-- and I know she'll love it.
Julia doesn't disappoint. "Do you remember me? I was in here yesterday and you wouldn't wait on me. You work on commission, right...?" Then the perfect pause as she holds up her arms, laden with shopping bags, and drops it like it's hot for those snotty shop girls...
"Big mistake. Big. HUGE! I have to go shopping now..."
My daughter grins. "Yeah. She's a baddie."
And so I call an Uber and take my pretty woman of a daughter to Rodeo Drive.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/35bec6db-cc5f-4bb5-9326-816b260dffbd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.TIXAykHLM9VWAgO2z9ftMl5H1yknY8h9Nd3jRiVd0U4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=185929429)
* * *
Rodeo Drive is tree-lined and pretty, but not as imposing as I expected.
Yes, we have to wait in line to cross velvet ropes into [Goyard](https://substack.com/redirect/db8d9ed9-e524-43ee-8e70-feac2bdb4eaa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and the well-suited shopkeepers greet us with nearly imperceptible sneers. But as a price of admission, it feels worth it -- the luxury wares are fun to visually fondle, a fleeting glimpse of how the other side lives.
We move on to Gucci, YSL, Tom Ford, and others, to similar results: luxurious leather goods, impeccable jewels and watches, and invisible price tags. If you have to ask -- don't.
The sunshine and sidewalks prove to be great equalizers, the sun warming our backs as we dodge dog poop strewn along the path. The shoppers of Rodeo clearly love their dogs, just as we love ours. Do they not believe in picking up after them? Even inside these highest-end shops, we see loads of dogs. I'd never bring our dog Kira into a store selling handbags worth more than my car, but sure -- let's pretend a wagging tail couldn't turn into a five-figure accident.
I spot a man carrying what look like conjoined white mini-poodles while his presumed wife is escorted through Hermes by several staff members. I smile and ask, "May I say hi to your dogs?" as he passes. He barely acknowledges me and keeps pace with the group, his dogs as indifferent to my offer of affection as he is to my very human question.
"Okay, I guess not," I mutter to his retreating back.
Then a fleeting thought: maybe he doesn't speak English. He isn't Edward from _Pretty Woman_ , even if he is paying the bill and carrying the dogs.
And then an observation: unlike _Pretty Woman_ , almost no one in the shop is white -- not the salespeople, and not the customers.
* * *
My daughter approaches me by the scarves. "No one in here will talk to me or even acknowledge me," she mutters.
This has never happened to my gorgeous, stylish, white child. Retail spaces have always been welcoming -- sometimes aggressively so.
"This is why _Pretty Woman_ is timeless," I tell her. We've all had that moment of being misjudged or dismissed by someone snotty.
"Imagine what it's like to be Black and have this happen everywhere," I add.
She rolls her eyes, and I deserve it for making it a lesson so fast. She already understands. But after today, I think she understands more deeply.
"It doesn't feel great," she says.
"No, it really doesn't," I say. I feel it too -- the judgment, the coolness, the disrespect. I'm not used to it either. Even though I don't care what they're judging me for, my skin isn't that thick. It gives me the ick, too.
"To be fair," I offer, "they probably clocked us as tourists, not customers. There's no way I was dropping thousands on a bag. This is how they make their living. I'm sure they've learned to be discerning."
"But they don't have to be rude about it," she counters.
She's not wrong.
And it gets me thinking: why were they so sure we couldn't afford it? I'm a late-middle-aged white woman. I have the credit limit. I have the good sense not to spend that kind of money on a logo -- but if I wanted to, I could.
Isn't Rodeo Drive supposed to be for people like me?
So what was it about me that made them so certain I didn't belong?
* * *
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=185929429&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1OTI5NDI5LCJpYXQiOjE3Njk5NjUxMTksImV4cCI6MTc3MjU1NzExOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7bNzhj9l1wbwwf5XcKiGx3nOV6Sn3hrfJPC0PQQCl04)
* * *
The answer, of course, is obvious: they've seen thousands of people like us walk through their doors. They've learned how to read bodies, clothes, posture, and confidence. They've learned how to profile. Their livelihoods depend on it. And they resent those of us who enter as tourists, not customers. That's totally fair, overt even.
What's less obvious -- and a shift from the world of _Pretty Woman_ just a few decades ago -- is what that profile looks like now.
And from what I observed, white people are no longer the default image of disposable income on Rodeo Drive.
The customers moving easily past the velvet ropes, the ones being ushered from counter to counter, are mostly brown or Asian. Many are speaking languages other than English. They arrived to Rodeo already wearing Christian Dior sneakers and Chanel sunglasses.
This is not a story about poverty. It's a story about power changing hands.
The fantasy of _Pretty Woman_ depended on a very specific idea of wealth: white, conservative, American, male. But that world is fading. The face of luxury has changed.
And now, we're the ones being snubbed.
* * *

* * *
I expected Minneapolis to weigh heavily on me all day.
What I didn't expect was this: Rodeo Drive and Minneapolis were telling the same story. And what connects the two isn't coincidence -- it's backlash.
On Rodeo Drive, I saw a future America already here: wealth and access no longer concentrated in white hands. The people moving most easily through those luxury spaces were largely brown and Asian, a visible sign that economic power is shifting.
In Minneapolis, I saw what happens when political power tries to claw back control over a country that no longer looks the way it used to. Immigration raids and militarized enforcement aren't just about borders. They are about reasserting dominance over bodies that represent change.
That's why these two scenes belong in the same story. One shows who is rising. The other shows how force fills the gap when legitimacy slips. On Rodeo Drive, we cordon off luxury from the non-wealthy. In Minneapolis, we enforce control over those labeled threats.
The settings differ, but the message is the same: _safety is selective_ , and force -- whether explicit through tear gas or implied through velvet ropes -- is the tool that keeps it that way.
On Rodeo Drive, that logic meant we were ignored by sales clerks.
In Minneapolis, that same logic turned deadly for Alex Pretti -- a white man who defied that reassertion of dominance, who intervened when others were targeted.
When fear governs power, it does not stop with the people it claims to hate. It expands outward. And anyone who steps into its path becomes expendable.
So the question isn't whether Minneapolis is an outlier.
It's whether it's a preview.
* * *
[Leave a comment](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=185929429&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1OTI5NDI5LCJpYXQiOjE3Njk5NjUxMTksImV4cCI6MTc3MjU1NzExOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7bNzhj9l1wbwwf5XcKiGx3nOV6Sn3hrfJPC0PQQCl04&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment)
* * *
After observing Rodeo Drive, here's what I do know: white Americans are no longer the default class among the most financially elite. We're not the dominant consumers of ultra-luxury. Sure, we may take Ubers to get there. But most of our drivers this weekend? GenX white men, grumbling about how cool things used to be in LA and how much it sucks now.
It's the same anger Trump's politics thrives on -- the fear of losing dominance in a country that no longer centers them.
This is the fear MAGA feeds on.
If we keep turning blue cities into battlegrounds, we only harden that fear -- and make places of possibility feel dangerous. Cities are where the top earners and innovators are. Some of them are white. Many of them are not. If you make these places dangerous, the talent and money will leave.
That would be a big mistake. Big. _Huge_.
We don't need to "do more shopping." We need to do more voting. We need to make sure we don't build velvet ropes around democracy itself.
* * *
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=185929429&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1OTI5NDI5LCJpYXQiOjE3Njk5NjUxMTksImV4cCI6MTc3MjU1NzExOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7bNzhj9l1wbwwf5XcKiGx3nOV6Sn3hrfJPC0PQQCl04)
* * *
I'll always have a soft spot for Prada, for the kindness only Adom showed us that day.
"You two are so cool and casual, I figured you were from LA," he says. "The locals always dress chill when they shop."
I know he's humoring us. With my wild red hair and sparkly cat-eye glasses, I don't look SoCal at all. Neither does my daughter in her UGG slides. But I appreciate that he gave her a good first Rodeo experience, so I play along.
"Thanks so much for your help," I tell him. "We might be back for this one."
My daughter's eyes widen, horrified. "Even I know not to spend that kind of money on a handbag," she whispers. I nod, and her face softens; we're in on the joke together.
We take one last look at the $2,600 bag -- lush burgundy leather, the little Prada triangle blazing on the front -- and step back outside the velvet rope and into the world.
We dodge the dog poop on the sidewalk, with a new appreciation of what gets cleaned up and what doesn't.
The luxury remains protected.
People do not.
