clean_body: What a college trip to Los Angeles revealed about who America protects--and who it's willing to sacrifice. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? What a college trip to Los Angeles revealed about who America protects--and who it's willing to sacrifice. | | and | Feb 1 | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Who does America protect? Rodeo Drive and a tear-gassed protest tell very different stories about safety and power. | | ---|---|--- Blue Amp Media is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. "Well, we do have college to pay for, but we also need to live a little, right?" I say, smiling at Adom in response to the price he's just revealed for the adorable Prada evening bag my daughter has looped over her forearm. $2,600. For a handbag. I catch the flash of disbelief on my daughter's face, even as I keep mine -- if not beatific, then at least placid. It isn't hard to smile. Adom is an adorable 30-something salesman who loves his job and treats us with nothing but kindness and good humor-- showering my eighteen-year-old with handbags to try on and gushing about what's great about each one. She's delighted. "Absolutely! You've gotta live it up!" he says. He means it, and most days, I do too. But today, my capacity for joy is blunted by the news I read just hours earlier. Across the country, another 30-something man didn't get to "live it up" -- or even to live. He was in Minneapolis during a federal immigration enforcement operation, trying to help someone nearby, and he didn't come home. The man was Alex Pretti, a 37-year-old ICU nurse at the Veterans Affairs hospital who cared deeply for the most injured and vulnerable patients. Witnesses and video footage suggest he was filming and trying to assist others when federal agents opened fire. The circumstances of his death have sparked widespread protests and outrage. I'm angry. I'm scared. My heart feels heavy. I'm supposed to be present for this precious time with my daughter. I'm also supposed to teach her, to lead by example. "Mom, put your phone down and live in the moment," she says. It's good advice. Is it good advice today? We walk on down Rodeo Drive. Every store is flawless, guarded, designed -- surreal in its retail grandeur. I sigh as we continue our walk, observing the handsome men in immaculate designer suits standing beside velvet ropes in front of every store. They aren't there to sell handbags. They're there to decide who gets inside. There's more visible security protecting leather goods than I've seen protecting people in our cities. Who do we show up for, I wonder. In the United States right now, the answer feels clear: we protect products over people. We no longer reliably honor our inalienable rights unless they come with a velvet rope and a logo. It's fun to window-shop on Rodeo, to marvel at all the beautiful things. But I feel the contrast tightening in my gut. Luxury is safe. A man is dead. And I keep circling the same question: who -- or what -- does America protect now? My daughter and I are in Los Angeles for yet another college tour. This school has offered her a generous scholarship, including a travel budget to come see the campus. So here we are. On the flight down, she announces, "I know I'm going to love LA." But she's lukewarm on the school itself, to my surprise. "Give it a chance," I tell her. "Tour it with an open mind." It's a good school, and the idea of her living on the West Coast, in a blue state, feels like a comfort to me. Or does it? The siege in Minneapolis has been going on for more than a week. I've never been there, but I have a close friend from Seattle who is, and know several others who have moved from Seattle to Minneapolis. I've long thought of us as sister cities -- big blue dots, liberal politics, weather most people find unappealing but those of us who choose it love, and a shared GenX soundtrack of 90s bands and . Even Cameron Crowe seemed to get it: the Singles was comprised almost entirely of Seattle bands plus Paul Westerberg of the Replacements -- a Minneapolis icon. We're not the same, but we're kin. I'm hyper-aware that where Minneapolis goes, Seattle may follow. They're known for niceness; we're known for the Seattle Freeze, coffee, and heroin. Neither city is famous for fighting. But those Minneapolis folks? They're warriors. Heroes. I see it now. They're out in subzero temperatures. They're literally whistleblowing to warn their neighbors. And in a city with deep Northern European roots, they're rising up to protect everyone -- especially their brown and Black neighbors. Will Seattle do the same? I don't know. I hope so. Could I put myself on the front lines and risk tear gas, arrest, or even death at the hands of the worst of what our nation can unleash: a scrambling gang of angry, poorly trained, overpaid, and over-empowered bullies who provoke, detain, and disappear people without just cause or consequence? I'm not sure I have that flavor of bravery. And could I live with my child alone in a faraway city and forced to do the same? Absolutely not. But I know this much: I need to get clear -- fast -- about what I'm willing to do, and what I'm willing to risk. They could be waiting for us when we get home. And my child has a college decision looming. I'm not surprised my daughter does, indeed, love LA. She has always been brand- and beauty-conscious. It isn't who I am, but she isn't me, and that's okay. I don't see it as a moral failing when she wants to visit Erewhon to see the (sold out) or drink the (delicious, like most strawberry-banana smoothies). I see her as aligned with her Gen Z peers, and sometimes, I indulge these interests. After a day wandering Santa Monica Pier and Venice Beach, she is ready for glamour. "Mom, I want to see fancy LA," she says. I look up from the grim news out of Minneapolis. Of course she wants this. And of course, we have to keep moving. Spending the day buried in headlines won't bring Alex back or make the protests safer. My daughter still deserves her vacation day, and I'm determined to give it to her. Of course, Rodeo Drive it is. She's never seen Pretty Woman , so I insist we watch a few iconic scenes in our hotel room first. I watch an impossibly young Julia Roberts clomp down Rodeo in her iconic white-and-blue hooker outfit. It was a star-making role. My daughter is awestruck. "She's so beautiful!" She's only known Julia as the mom in Wonder , not as the devastating wild-haired ingenue she once was. In the clip, Julia's character enters a Rodeo Drive shop asking for "something conservative" and is dismissed with a prim, "I don't think we have anything for you. You're obviously in the wrong place." The shop clerks are stuffy, well-coiffed, tightly buttoned -- more like Southern matrons than the style icons my daughter expects. Our YouTube clip skips ahead to the shopping montage, set to Roy Orbison's "Pretty Woman." "Those clothes are super ugly," my daughter opines. She's not wrong. They're of their era, but it's a conservative slice of it -- prudish, matronly, nothing that appeals to a modern teen or, frankly, to me. "But wait for it," I tell her. The line is coming -- you know the line -- and I know she'll love it. Julia doesn't disappoint. "Do you remember me? I was in here yesterday and you wouldn't wait on me. You work on commission, right...?" Then the perfect pause as she holds up her arms, laden with shopping bags, and drops it like it's hot for those snotty shop girls... "Big mistake. Big. HUGE! I have to go shopping now..." My daughter grins. "Yeah. She's a baddie." And so I call an Uber and take my pretty woman of a daughter to Rodeo Drive. Rodeo Drive is tree-lined and pretty, but not as imposing as I expected. Yes, we have to wait in line to cross velvet ropes into , and the well-suited shopkeepers greet us with nearly imperceptible sneers. But as a price of admission, it feels worth it -- the luxury wares are fun to visually fondle, a fleeting glimpse of how the other side lives. We move on to Gucci, YSL, Tom Ford, and others, to similar results: luxurious leather goods, impeccable jewels and watches, and invisible price tags. If you have to ask -- don't. The sunshine and sidewalks prove to be great equalizers, the sun warming our backs as we dodge dog poop strewn along the path. The shoppers of Rodeo clearly love their dogs, just as we love ours. Do they not believe in picking up after them? Even inside these highest-end shops, we see loads of dogs. I'd never bring our dog Kira into a store selling handbags worth more than my car, but sure -- let's pretend a wagging tail couldn't turn into a five-figure accident. I spot a man carrying what look like conjoined white mini-poodles while his presumed wife is escorted through Hermes by several staff members. I smile and ask, "May I say hi to your dogs?" as he passes. He barely acknowledges me and keeps pace with the group, his dogs as indifferent to my offer of affection as he is to my very human question. "Okay, I guess not," I mutter to his retreating back. Then a fleeting thought: maybe he doesn't speak English. He isn't Edward from Pretty Woman , even if he is paying the bill and carrying the dogs. And then an observation: unlike Pretty Woman , almost no one in the shop is white -- not the salespeople, and not the customers. My daughter approaches me by the scarves. "No one in here will talk to me or even acknowledge me," she mutters. This has never happened to my gorgeous, stylish, white child. Retail spaces have always been welcoming -- sometimes aggressively so. "This is why Pretty Woman is timeless," I tell her. We've all had that moment of being misjudged or dismissed by someone snotty. "Imagine what it's like to be Black and have this happen everywhere," I add. She rolls her eyes, and I deserve it for making it a lesson so fast. She already understands. But after today, I think she understands more deeply. "It doesn't feel great," she says. "No, it really doesn't," I say. I feel it too -- the judgment, the coolness, the disrespect. I'm not used to it either. Even though I don't care what they're judging me for, my skin isn't that thick. It gives me the ick, too. "To be fair," I offer, "they probably clocked us as tourists, not customers. There's no way I was dropping thousands on a bag. This is how they make their living. I'm sure they've learned to be discerning." "But they don't have to be rude about it," she counters. She's not wrong. And it gets me thinking: why were they so sure we couldn't afford it? I'm a late-middle-aged white woman. I have the credit limit. I have the good sense not to spend that kind of money on a logo -- but if I wanted to, I could. Isn't Rodeo Drive supposed to be for people like me? So what was it about me that made them so certain I didn't belong? The answer, of course, is obvious: they've seen thousands of people like us walk through their doors. They've learned how to read bodies, clothes, posture, and confidence. They've learned how to profile. Their livelihoods depend on it. And they resent those of us who enter as tourists, not customers. That's totally fair, overt even. What's less obvious -- and a shift from the world of Pretty Woman just a few decades ago -- is what that profile looks like now. And from what I observed, white people are no longer the default image of disposable income on Rodeo Drive. The customers moving easily past the velvet ropes, the ones being ushered from counter to counter, are mostly brown or Asian. Many are speaking languages other than English. They arrived to Rodeo already wearing Christian Dior sneakers and Chanel sunglasses. This is not a story about poverty. It's a story about power changing hands. The fantasy of Pretty Woman depended on a very specific idea of wealth: white, conservative, American, male. But that world is fading. The face of luxury has changed. And now, we're the ones being snubbed. Blue Amp Media is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. I expected Minneapolis to weigh heavily on me all day. What I didn't expect was this: Rodeo Drive and Minneapolis were telling the same story. And what connects the two isn't coincidence -- it's backlash. On Rodeo Drive, I saw a future America already here: wealth and access no longer concentrated in white hands. The people moving most easily through those luxury spaces were largely brown and Asian, a visible sign that economic power is shifting. In Minneapolis, I saw what happens when political power tries to claw back control over a country that no longer looks the way it used to. Immigration raids and militarized enforcement aren't just about borders. They are about reasserting dominance over bodies that represent change. That's why these two scenes belong in the same story. One shows who is rising. The other shows how force fills the gap when legitimacy slips. On Rodeo Drive, we cordon off luxury from the non-wealthy. In Minneapolis, we enforce control over those labeled threats. The settings differ, but the message is the same: safety is selective , and force -- whether explicit through tear gas or implied through velvet ropes -- is the tool that keeps it that way. On Rodeo Drive, that logic meant we were ignored by sales clerks. In Minneapolis, that same logic turned deadly for Alex Pretti -- a white man who defied that reassertion of dominance, who intervened when others were targeted. When fear governs power, it does not stop with the people it claims to hate. It expands outward. And anyone who steps into its path becomes expendable. So the question isn't whether Minneapolis is an outlier. It's whether it's a preview. After observing Rodeo Drive, here's what I do know: white Americans are no longer the default class among the most financially elite. We're not the dominant consumers of ultra-luxury. Sure, we may take Ubers to get there. But most of our drivers this weekend? GenX white men, grumbling about how cool things used to be in LA and how much it sucks now. It's the same anger Trump's politics thrives on -- the fear of losing dominance in a country that no longer centers them. This is the fear MAGA feeds on. If we keep turning blue cities into battlegrounds, we only harden that fear -- and make places of possibility feel dangerous. Cities are where the top earners and innovators are. Some of them are white. Many of them are not. If you make these places dangerous, the talent and money will leave. That would be a big mistake. Big. Huge. We don't need to "do more shopping." We need to do more voting. We need to make sure we don't build velvet ropes around democracy itself. I'll always have a soft spot for Prada, for the kindness only Adom showed us that day. "You two are so cool and casual, I figured you were from LA," he says. "The locals always dress chill when they shop." I know he's humoring us. With my wild red hair and sparkly cat-eye glasses, I don't look SoCal at all. Neither does my daughter in her UGG slides. But I appreciate that he gave her a good first Rodeo experience, so I play along. "Thanks so much for your help," I tell him. "We might be back for this one." My daughter's eyes widen, horrified. "Even I know not to spend that kind of money on a handbag," she whispers. I nod, and her face softens; we're in on the joke together. We take one last look at the $2,600 bag -- lush burgundy leather, the little Prada triangle blazing on the front -- and step back outside the velvet rope and into the world. We dodge the dog poop on the sidewalk, with a new appreciation of what gets cleaned up and what doesn't. The luxury remains protected. People do not. | | ---|---|--- Greetings! I'm , a GenX word nerd living in the Pacific Northwest with a whole lot of little words to share. I'm the co-host of and Editorial & Booking Director here at . I write across a variety of topics but parenting, music and pop culture, relationships, and feminism are my favorites. Em-dashes, Oxford commas, and well-placed semi-colons make my heart happy. If this story resonated with you, why not ? (Paying for college is expensive!) | | ---|---|--- Me on Santa Monica beach, the day before Alex Pretti's murder and our Rodeo Drive adventure. Read More Blue Amp Media Want to go deeper? These Blue Amp Media stories explore the same themes of accountability, courage, and democratic renewal from different angles. | | and | | ---|---|--- | Jan 28 | | | and | | ---|---|--- | Jan 29 | You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Cliff Schecter PO Box 8384, Cincinnati, OH 45208 ? ?

name: Amanda K. Edwards
subject: Last Night's Results
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Amanda Edwards for Congress
body:
https://www.edwardsforhouston.com/ [https://www.edwardsforhouston.com/]
Friend,
While last night's results were not what we hoped for, I am deeply grateful to my team and every volunteer, resident, and supporter who believed in this campaign and worked tirelessly to build a grassroots movement to place people over politics in our community. Across this district, voters made clear they are ready to reject extremism and demand leadership that delivers real results for working families.
I congratulate Christian Menefee on his victory. As the Trump Administration continues to attack our jobs, our schools, and our access to health care, I will keep standing up for this community and pushing for real, common-sense solutions. This fight does not end.
Thank you.
Amanda K. Edwards
[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/specialelectionrunoffparty?refcode2=50c3e410-88ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c]

name: Roy Cooper
subject: Asking for your endorsement — not your dollar
body:
This is one of the best ways to say you’re with us.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM)
[](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/endorse-roy-cooper/?source=em_RC_PET_EndorseRAC_020126&t=7&akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM) Hello Peter, I really can’t begin to tell you how much it means to me every time someone chooses to support our campaign for the U.S. Senate. Whether that’s by investing in our work with a contribution, sharing our news or updates online, or telling friends and neighbors about us — your support is what will help us succeed. **[Peter, one of the most effective ways to say you’re with us is by endorsing our campaign, and I’d like to personally invite you to do so today. If you’re in, just click here to share your endorsement.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/endorse-roy-cooper/?source=em_RC_PET_EndorseRAC_020126&t=8&akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM)** | [ENDORSE ROY COOPER](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/endorse-roy-cooper/?source=em_RC_PET_EndorseRAC_020126&t=9&akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM)
---
Thank you so much for your support, now and through the months ahead. This is the most competitive race I’ve ever run, and I couldn’t do it without you.
Gratefully,
Roy
[](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/endorse-roy-cooper/?source=em_RC_PET_EndorseRAC_020126&t=10&akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM)
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM)**
[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=3&akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM)
---
[DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1915?source=&t=4&akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM)
[SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=5&akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=6&akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.roycooper.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM)
If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=11&akid=457%2E124072%2EXBUytM)

subject: A Note on Hope
body:
By Marilyn Kunce

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.eAJWrbANfgOSO8OKvVXo0ybzqwJ-QeVMzmNJWZPR8as?) for more

[](https://substack..af_Z9JVwvOYjBGYmfvO_zvQBHBg2qKuOnU19HVX8lhs?)
---
# [A Note on Hope](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=186510041&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NTEwMDQxLCJpYXQiOjE3Njk5NjI0OTksImV4cCI6MTc3MjU1NDQ5OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Z0b3rSTa_xhRpdz4ZetfW_p0m7ORqpXMnwn35hsVQL0)
### By Marilyn Kunce
Feb 1
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=186510041&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NTEwMDQxLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2OTk2MjQ5OSwiZXhwIjoxNzcyNTU0NDk5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTg2Nzc0MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.YWNIB-zgk32W0SRjfIvuSmob2j_GyIRKH7rzBqH-zdU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=186510041&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NTEwMDQxLCJpYXQiOjE3Njk5NjI0OTksImV4cCI6MTc3MjU1NDQ5OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Z0b3rSTa_xhRpdz4ZetfW_p0m7ORqpXMnwn35hsVQL0&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=186510041&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg2NTEwMDQxLCJpYXQiOjE3Njk5NjI0OTksImV4cCI6MTc3MjU1NDQ5OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Z0b3rSTa_xhRpdz4ZetfW_p0m7ORqpXMnwn35hsVQL0)
---
| | [](https://substack..07iTeaNfCwD8mSqGbHZmkmE4YK1nBWHT-Zbhsa6EFDY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/lucaskunce/p/a-note-on-hope?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

These last few weeks have been heavy.
As you may know, I am Mexican. And anyone who looks like me or sounds like me has been scared to go out for a while now, even if, like me, they have done everything by the book. Because "aggressive immigration enforcement" is actually just code for racial profiling. And we are all targets. In Minnesota, police chiefs have been speaking out because they are tired of their off-duty members being illegally harassed by ICE. Proving what we already knew, they report that [every single one of their people who have been stopped is a person of color](https://substack.com/redirect/96d2a3f2-1b66-4a75-b50b-52f2805696a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
It's incredibly hard on parents.
Anyone with kids in this country, like me, who are mixed race or who speak their parents' native language, is scared to keep teaching them their traditions.
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.NWzlPdhpACIX8oufFCQnlDxN41vFsIgTrRZbq0lGPxQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=186510041)
When children are heard screaming on the other side of immigration detention facilities, in unsafe and inhumane conditions such as [limited access to clean drinking water, spoiled or contaminated food, and inadequate medical care,](https://substack.com/redirect/25756502-330b-4a0a-95e1-aa8b18317f07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) it becomes impossible to look at your own children without worrying that they could be taken away, regardless of whether they are citizens and you are here legally or not.
If you want to talk about crimes being committed, lets talk about the crime against humanity that is detaining and separating children from their families. Especially when [investigations have shown that federal agencies lack any comprehensive system to track detainees across transfers, leaving families desperate for answers after the kidnapping has occurred.](https://substack.com/redirect/9a66806c-bd09-4e78-b5f9-05205706f527?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
On top of holding my kids tighter, I want to thank every single one of you who are out there fighting this. Standing up for humanity, decency, and what is right. Fighting for the American way that is under attack. Risking your lives to stand up against something that is so patently wrong.
The idea of separation on its own is terrifying. I work from home. My kids are used to seeing their mom nearly 24 hours a day. If I disappeared from one day to the next, I am certain it would be deeply traumatizing for them.
But it becomes even more horrifying when you see what is actually happening. Disgusting reports of an immigration [officer sexually abusing a mother in exchange for allowing her to see her detained daughter.](https://substack.com/redirect/d552cb47-e130-469f-badf-5b9cd351bbc0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) An abuse of government power against someone who was completely vulnerable.
Does that woman have a husband? Do those who claim this cruelty is God ordained believe her desperation was a sin too? Because what happened was not moral. It was coercion. It was violence.
[People go missing in these facilities all the time.](https://substack.com/redirect/3b35eaf4-d535-4795-a4be-bd15ca83dcf3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) And there is no reliable public accounting of how many people have died in immigration custody or have been transferred across borders without families being able to locate them.
So thank you again, because seeing Americans in the streets protesting and defending immigrants gives us all a sense that we are not alone, that Americans who can will use their voices when others can not. I am in awe at how brave you are. You are still out there, even after Renee Good [was shot and killed by a federal agent during immigration protests in Minneapolis](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9a73cf-046f-4545-a7eb-ad1b8a8b6374?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Alex Pretti was killed](https://substack.com/redirect/b1cb334e-c426-493d-87c8-c1d153dde523?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) while standing between armed agents and a woman who was being surrounded and forced to the ground.
Alex was an ICU nurse who cared for veterans. He was a son, a friend, and a member of his community who spent his life easing others' pain.
Renee was a mother. She had three children waiting for her at home.
They didn't have to be there, but they were, standing up for the country they love and the decency it used to have.
I want everyone to be safe. I feel sickened by this uncalled-for violence. [These deaths are part of a growing list of immigration enforcement related fatalities](https://substack.com/redirect/4ee121de-6ca6-4a66-829b-c11d01392140?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) documented this year alone.
The violence continues in Minnesota, and all I want is safety for everyone involved. To those standing up for us, your bravery matters. It is making a difference, even when it feels impossibly heavy.
As many of you know, I had a baby last year. Lukie is the sweetest bundle of joy, and he and Harvey, along with their two older brothers, fill our days with chaos and laughter. I have not spoken much about the postpartum depression I have experienced since this pregnancy. I am fortunate to have a strong support system, an involved husband, family nearby, and the ability to care for my mental health. Recently, I started moving my body again, and that has helped more than I expected.
Today, I had a realization.
What is happening in this country around immigration is giving me debilitating anxiety. I have been pushing it aside, blaming lack of sleep, exhaustion, and postpartum depression. But the news, the killings, the apprehension of children, the lack of transparency and justice, all of it has been building inside me.
I am Mexican. I came here legally. I had a work visa. I did everything the so-called right way. I have written about [my immigration story](https://open.substack.com/pub/lucaskunce/p/the-american-dream?utm_campaign=post-expanded-share&utm_medium=web) before, but that is not what this piece is about.
The latest events in Minnesota sank my spirit in a way I had not felt before. And while I experience this from the safety of my home, with access to care, medication, and comfort, my mind is not a safe place. It is dark. Rage shows up sometimes, but mostly, hope feels like it is slipping away.
And then I realized something else.
This is exactly what fear and cruelty are meant to do. They want us exhausted. They want us silent. They want us numb.
We can not give in to that.
Do not give up to these forces.
Thank you for using your voices when others cannot. Your presence matters. Your courage matters. Please stay. We need you out there, protecting us
And we will need you now through election day. Lucas is not running this cycle, but there are so many who are running whose victories can change the course of history. More than anything, we need them to win so that we can turn the tide. Please help them.
Tonight, I will tuck my children in. I will speak my language to them. I will teach them where they come from. I will keep loving loudly in a world that wants us afraid. That is how I hold on to hope.
I am afraid. I am angry. I am still here. And I am not done hoping.
Hope does not look like optimism right now. It looks like refusal. Refusal to forget. Refusal to be silent. Refusal to let fear decide who we become.
Thank you for everything you do.
Marilyn Kunce

clean_body: By Marilyn Kunce | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? By Marilyn Kunce Feb 1 | | | | | | | | | | | These last few weeks have been heavy. As you may know, I am Mexican. And anyone who looks like me or sounds like me has been scared to go out for a while now, even if, like me, they have done everything by the book. Because "aggressive immigration enforcement" is actually just code for racial profiling. And we are all targets. In Minnesota, police chiefs have been speaking out because they are tired of their off-duty members being illegally harassed by ICE. Proving what we already knew, they report that . It's incredibly hard on parents. Anyone with kids in this country, like me, who are mixed race or who speak their parents' native language, is scared to keep teaching them their traditions. Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. When children are heard screaming on the other side of immigration detention facilities, in unsafe and inhumane conditions such as it becomes impossible to look at your own children without worrying that they could be taken away, regardless of whether they are citizens and you are here legally or not. If you want to talk about crimes being committed, lets talk about the crime against humanity that is detaining and separating children from their families. Especially when On top of holding my kids tighter, I want to thank every single one of you who are out there fighting this. Standing up for humanity, decency, and what is right. Fighting for the American way that is under attack. Risking your lives to stand up against something that is so patently wrong. The idea of separation on its own is terrifying. I work from home. My kids are used to seeing their mom nearly 24 hours a day. If I disappeared from one day to the next, I am certain it would be deeply traumatizing for them. But it becomes even more horrifying when you see what is actually happening. Disgusting reports of an immigration An abuse of government power against someone who was completely vulnerable. Does that woman have a husband? Do those who claim this cruelty is God ordained believe her desperation was a sin too? Because what happened was not moral. It was coercion. It was violence. And there is no reliable public accounting of how many people have died in immigration custody or have been transferred across borders without families being able to locate them. So thank you again, because seeing Americans in the streets protesting and defending immigrants gives us all a sense that we are not alone, that Americans who can will use their voices when others can not. I am in awe at how brave you are. You are still out there, even after Renee Good and while standing between armed agents and a woman who was being surrounded and forced to the ground. Alex was an ICU nurse who cared for veterans. He was a son, a friend, and a member of his community who spent his life easing others' pain. Renee was a mother. She had three children waiting for her at home. They didn't have to be there, but they were, standing up for the country they love and the decency it used to have. I want everyone to be safe. I feel sickened by this uncalled-for violence. documented this year alone. The violence continues in Minnesota, and all I want is safety for everyone involved. To those standing up for us, your bravery matters. It is making a difference, even when it feels impossibly heavy. As many of you know, I had a baby last year. Lukie is the sweetest bundle of joy, and he and Harvey, along with their two older brothers, fill our days with chaos and laughter. I have not spoken much about the postpartum depression I have experienced since this pregnancy. I am fortunate to have a strong support system, an involved husband, family nearby, and the ability to care for my mental health. Recently, I started moving my body again, and that has helped more than I expected. Today, I had a realization. What is happening in this country around immigration is giving me debilitating anxiety. I have been pushing it aside, blaming lack of sleep, exhaustion, and postpartum depression. But the news, the killings, the apprehension of children, the lack of transparency and justice, all of it has been building inside me. I am Mexican. I came here legally. I had a work visa. I did everything the so-called right way. I have written about before, but that is not what this piece is about. The latest events in Minnesota sank my spirit in a way I had not felt before. And while I experience this from the safety of my home, with access to care, medication, and comfort, my mind is not a safe place. It is dark. Rage shows up sometimes, but mostly, hope feels like it is slipping away. And then I realized something else. This is exactly what fear and cruelty are meant to do. They want us exhausted. They want us silent. They want us numb. We can not give in to that. Do not give up to these forces. Thank you for using your voices when others cannot. Your presence matters. Your courage matters. Please stay. We need you out there, protecting us And we will need you now through election day. Lucas is not running this cycle, but there are so many who are running whose victories can change the course of history. More than anything, we need them to win so that we can turn the tide. Please help them. Tonight, I will tuck my children in. I will speak my language to them. I will teach them where they come from. I will keep loving loudly in a world that wants us afraid. That is how I hold on to hope. I am afraid. I am angry. I am still here. And I am not done hoping. Hope does not look like optimism right now. It looks like refusal. Refusal to forget. Refusal to be silent. Refusal to let fear decide who we become. Thank you for everything you do. Marilyn Kunce Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. body:
We have a lot to cover.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124510155/598839792/1328794144?refcode=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ms=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjM3ZGRmMjI4LTg5ZmYtZjAxMS04MzJmLTAwMGQzYTFmMGU0YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=5TnNhRC4-aaJ9L72NtPNqvkqUQ6hrPyK_YvYVJwp9kQ=&emci=9b5c73a8-0dfe-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&emdi=37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ceid=4823105)
---
You're reading the February edition of **_The JanePAC Monthly,_** and if January taught us anything, it's this: _we're not backing down._ Jane took her message straight to millions of Americans on _The Late Show,_ our JanePAC climate champ, California Attorney General Rob Bonta, is taking Big Oil to court, and brave leaders like Peggy Flanagan are standing up to Trump's authoritarian and inhumane overreach in Minnesota. Let's dive in! 👇
* * *
**✊ JANE IN ACTION** | **The Late Show With Stephen Colbert**
---
Jane used her platform to sound the alarm about the growing threats to our democracy — and to remind millions of Americans that defending it is a shared responsibility.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124510156/598839793/-632870329?refcode=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ms=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjM3ZGRmMjI4LTg5ZmYtZjAxMS04MzJmLTAwMGQzYTFmMGU0YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=5TnNhRC4-aaJ9L72NtPNqvkqUQ6hrPyK_YvYVJwp9kQ=&emci=9b5c73a8-0dfe-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&emdi=37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ceid=4823105)
She warned that authoritarianism has crept into every corner of our federal government and spoke about the escalating violence and lawlessness unfolding in Minnesota.
**Jane closed with a call to action: organize in solidarity, build strong communities, and remember a simple truth — tyrants are never as powerful as they believe, and people are always stronger than they realize.**
[**SEE WHAT JANE HAD TO SAY →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124510157/598839794/-632870329?refcode=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ms=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjM3ZGRmMjI4LTg5ZmYtZjAxMS04MzJmLTAwMGQzYTFmMGU0YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=5TnNhRC4-aaJ9L72NtPNqvkqUQ6hrPyK_YvYVJwp9kQ=&emci=9b5c73a8-0dfe-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&emdi=37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ceid=4823105)
---
**How Jane Stays Strong at 88 (and how you can too!)**
---
Taking care of our bodies is part of caring for our movement; it’s how we stay strong enough to keep showing up. At 88, Jane makes movement a priority — _every single day._
From cardio and resistance training to simple squats that build strength and balance, Jane works with trainer Malin Svensson on slower, accessible movements anyone can do from the comfort of their home.
Jane knows that when it comes to fitness, age is just a number, and her simple approach proves that consistency matters far more than intensity.
**[Woman's World Magazine recently published an in-depth look at Jane's workout routine, featuring three of her go-to exercises broken down by physical therapist Dr. Milica McDowell. You can try them all at home today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124510158/598839795/1128742087?refcode=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ms=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjM3ZGRmMjI4LTg5ZmYtZjAxMS04MzJmLTAwMGQzYTFmMGU0YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=5TnNhRC4-aaJ9L72NtPNqvkqUQ6hrPyK_YvYVJwp9kQ=&emci=9b5c73a8-0dfe-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&emdi=37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ceid=4823105)**
[**GET JANE'S GO-TO MOVES →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124510159/598839796/1128742087?refcode=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ms=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjM3ZGRmMjI4LTg5ZmYtZjAxMS04MzJmLTAwMGQzYTFmMGU0YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=5TnNhRC4-aaJ9L72NtPNqvkqUQ6hrPyK_YvYVJwp9kQ=&emci=9b5c73a8-0dfe-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&emdi=37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ceid=4823105)
---
* * *
**🏆 JANEPAC CLIMATE CHAMPION UPDATE**
We love it when an Attorney General isn't afraid to take on Big Oil — that's exactly why JanePAC endorsed climate champion Rob Bonta for re-election!
Rob sued ExxonMobil, Shell, Chevron, ConocoPhillips, BP, and the American Petroleum Institute for covering up their role in the climate crisis. After decades of lying to the public while raking in record profits, these companies are _finally_ being held accountable.
He's also unleashed a flurry of lawsuits against the Trump administration over their attempts to freeze federal climate funding, roll back California's environmental protections, and halt offshore wind projects.
Every time Donald Trump breaks the law or hands Big Oil another giveaway, we need prosecutors like Rob holding him accountable — and this grassroots movement can help him win his re-election campaign. Stay tuned for more updates!
* * *
**⚠️ TRUMP IS ERASING AMERICAN HISTORY FROM OUR NATIONAL PARKS**
If you've visited a national park lately, you might notice something's missing. Park Service workers across the country are dismantling historical exhibits under Trump's new directive to remove content that may "_disparage Americans._ "
In Philadelphia, they tore down slavery memorials with crowbars. In California, climate science exhibits vanished. Films about workers' struggles stopped showing.
**“African American history is American history, and this is an intentional effort to erase history and whitewash...”**

_— Kenyatta Johnson, President of Philadelphia City Council (via The NYT)_
---
Make no mistake: **This is part of Trump's authoritarian playbook to erase the uncomfortable truths about America's past so no one can use them to challenge power in the present.**
[**READ THE FULL STORY →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124510160/598839797/-640013036?refcode=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ms=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjM3ZGRmMjI4LTg5ZmYtZjAxMS04MzJmLTAwMGQzYTFmMGU0YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=5TnNhRC4-aaJ9L72NtPNqvkqUQ6hrPyK_YvYVJwp9kQ=&emci=9b5c73a8-0dfe-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&emdi=37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ceid=4823105)
---
* * *
**🌎 WE NEED LEADERS LIKE PEGGY FLANAGAN**
Trump's cruel ICE operations in Minnesota and authoritarian overreach have made it clear: we desperately need leaders who refuse to sit on the sidelines while he consolidates power.
**That's why JanePAC endorsed Peggy Flanagan for U.S. Senate in Minnesota last month — and Peggy has already proven she’s not only consistent in her values, she’s looking Trump's cruel and hateful agenda in the eye and saying _no, not in Minnesota._**
She's protested ICE raids alongside Minnesotans, championed 100% clean energy, and enacted a law as Lieutenant Governor to require consultation with Tribes on climate policies.
This is a make-or-break year for both our planet and our democracy — and sitting back while Trump’s cruelty unfolds has never been more dangerous. Peggy is putting everything on the line to defend Minnesota from Trump's attacks, and new polling released this week shows she holds a commanding 13-point lead (49%-36%) over Congresswoman Angie Craig in Minnesota’s Democratic U.S. Senate primary.
**[Momentum is on our side, but only if we keep showing up. Will you split a donation today between Peggy's campaign and JanePAC so we can elect more leaders like her who refuse to back down when it matters most?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124510161/598839798/89281171?refcode=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ms=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjM3ZGRmMjI4LTg5ZmYtZjAxMS04MzJmLTAwMGQzYTFmMGU0YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=5TnNhRC4-aaJ9L72NtPNqvkqUQ6hrPyK_YvYVJwp9kQ=)**
[**SUPPORT PEGGY & JANEPAC →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124510162/598839799/89281171?refcode=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ms=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjM3ZGRmMjI4LTg5ZmYtZjAxMS04MzJmLTAwMGQzYTFmMGU0YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=5TnNhRC4-aaJ9L72NtPNqvkqUQ6hrPyK_YvYVJwp9kQ=)
---
* * *
That's it for this edition of **_The JanePAC Monthly_**.
Thank you for being part of this movement. If you want to stay plugged in between newsletters, follow us on social media for daily updates and behind-the-scenes moments with Jane and the team:
[**INSTAGRAM**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124510163/598839800/458897881?refcode=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ms=ea.37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjM3ZGRmMjI4LTg5ZmYtZjAxMS04MzJmLTAwMGQzYTFmMGU0YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=5TnNhRC4-aaJ9L72NtPNqvkqUQ6hrPyK_YvYVJwp9kQ=&emci=9b5c73a8-0dfe-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&emdi=37ddf228-89ff-f011-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ceid=4823105)

clean_body: We have a lot to cover. | You're reading the February edition of The JanePAC Monthly, and if January taught us anything, it's this: we're not backing down. Jane took her message straight to millions of Americans on The Late Show, our JanePAC climate champ, California Attorney General Rob Bonta, is taking Big Oil to court, and brave leaders like Peggy Flanagan are standing up to Trump's authoritarian and inhumane overreach in Minnesota. Let's dive in! 👇 ✊ JANE IN ACTION | The Late Show With Stephen Colbert Jane used her platform to sound the alarm about the growing threats to our democracy — and to remind millions of Americans that defending it is a shared responsibility. She warned that authoritarianism has crept into every corner of our federal government and spoke about the escalating violence and lawlessness unfolding in Minnesota. Jane closed with a call to action: organize in solidarity, build strong communities, and remember a simple truth — tyrants are never as powerful as they believe, and people are always stronger than they realize. How Jane Stays Strong at 88 (and how you can too!) Taking care of our bodies is part of caring for our movement; it’s how we stay strong enough to keep showing up. At 88, Jane makes movement a priority — every single day. From cardio and resistance training to simple squats that build strength and balance, Jane works with trainer Malin Svensson on slower, accessible movements anyone can do from the comfort of their home. Jane knows that when it comes to fitness, age is just a number, and her simple approach proves that consistency matters far more than intensity. 🏆 JANEPAC CLIMATE CHAMPION UPDATE We love it when an Attorney General isn't afraid to take on Big Oil — that's exactly why JanePAC endorsed climate champion Rob Bonta for re-election! Rob sued ExxonMobil, Shell, Chevron, ConocoPhillips, BP, and the American Petroleum Institute for covering up their role in the climate crisis. After decades of lying to the public while raking in record profits, these companies are finally being held accountable. He's also unleashed a flurry of lawsuits against the Trump administration over their attempts to freeze federal climate funding, roll back California's environmental protections, and halt offshore wind projects. Every time Donald Trump breaks the law or hands Big Oil another giveaway, we need prosecutors like Rob holding him accountable — and this grassroots movement can help him win his re-election campaign. Stay tuned for more updates! ⚠️ TRUMP IS ERASING AMERICAN HISTORY FROM OUR NATIONAL PARKS If you've visited a national park lately, you might notice something's missing. Park Service workers across the country are dismantling historical exhibits under Trump's new directive to remove content that may "disparage Americans. " In Philadelphia, they tore down slavery memorials with crowbars. In California, climate science exhibits vanished. Films about workers' struggles stopped showing. “African American history is American history, and this is an intentional effort to erase history and whitewash...” — Kenyatta Johnson, President of Philadelphia City Council (via The NYT) Make no mistake: This is part of Trump's authoritarian playbook to erase the uncomfortable truths about America's past so no one can use them to challenge power in the present. 🌎 WE NEED LEADERS LIKE PEGGY FLANAGAN Trump's cruel ICE operations in Minnesota and authoritarian overreach have made it clear: we desperately need leaders who refuse to sit on the sidelines while he consolidates power. That's why JanePAC endorsed Peggy Flanagan for U.S. Senate in Minnesota last month — and Peggy has already proven she’s not only consistent in her values, she’s looking Trump's cruel and hateful agenda in the eye and saying no, not in Minnesota. She's protested ICE raids alongside Minnesotans, championed 100% clean energy, and enacted a law as Lieutenant Governor to require consultation with Tribes on climate policies. This is a make-or-break year for both our planet and our democracy — and sitting back while Trump’s cruelty unfolds has never been more dangerous. Peggy is putting everything on the line to defend Minnesota from Trump's attacks, and new polling released this week shows she holds a commanding 13-point lead (49%-36%) over Congresswoman Angie Craig in Minnesota’s Democratic U.S. Senate primary. = = That's it for this edition of The JanePAC Monthly. Thank you for being part of this movement. If you want to stay plugged in between newsletters, follow us on social media for daily updates and behind-the-scenes moments with Jane and the team: Together, we are building a movement they cannot ignore. And we're just getting started. In solidarity, – Team JanePAC = | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Send donations via check to: Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

clean_body: We’re entering the new month in full force, Peter. | = Hi Peter, We’re only a month into 2026, and we’ve already proven that we’re not going gradually into this election year – we’re coming in with full force. From endorsements to organizing, the work to elect Democratic pro-choice women is well underway. But this is just the beginning. Here at EMILYs List, we’re putting in the effort now to help our women build power early in the cycle. = | = Here’s what’s coming up: !!️ Endorsements are rolling in — Amy Klobuchar(MN-GOV),Mary Peltola (AK-SEN), and more joined our slate last month, and we don’t plan on slowing down anytime soon. Our Democratic pro-choice women are stepping up in some of the nation’s most critical races and are ready to deliver. 🗣️ It’s debate season —** As EMILYs List women gear up for their primaries, our team is working on the ground to make sure they have the resources they need to succeed. From debate prep to rapid response, we’re working behind the scenes to ensure our candidates are ready to take on their opponents and connect with voters. 🪪 There’s still time to secure your 2026 membership — We’ll be launching our second membership drive of the year in a couple weeks, so don’t miss out on the opportunity to pledge your support! Keep an eye on your inbox for more details and get your own personalized card. We’ve got a lot to look forward to this month. Every action we take is building momentum for Election Day 2026. But we can only make this happen with your continued support. =** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you, the EMILYs List Campaigns team | = | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. Friend, People are pushing back against ICE across the country, and they need our support. In Arizona, State Senator Analise Ortiz is facing attacks from MAGA Republicans for sharing a social media post that alerted her constituents to ICE’s presence outside of an elementary school. In addition to calling on the U.S. Attorney’s Office to investigate Sen. Ortiz, Arizona Republicans have also introduced legislation that would make it a felony to “interfere” with ICE activity—even citing Sen. Ortiz’s Instagram post. **Let’s show State Senator Analise Ortiz that we have her back in the face of MAGA Republicans’ pro-ICE attacks, including a U.S. Attorney investigation, an ethics committee hearing and investigation, and legislation in her state seeking to silence dissent.** [**Split a donation between Sen. Ortiz’s campaign and my re-election campaign today to hold ICE accountable and push back against Republicans who endanger our communities and seek to silence our movement for justice.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJUNGGCt2LXO_wl_jAVvqUvh6EUwWzbGD8QCJ1ywXtqVVA4h3vGVp4lk0DTy99oKWDlpB8Qn5hI_NoXDQKCEyiRXa3ZTlJDbi7lauIZngbbFcRr6V1f5DBiZ8PfOKKx1bowTDK7FiNVkkjVPupoIl8JTyQYPMnU-kOJ73Z60XHUkUXHYmIz37Jl3okF8pVoxOwVt_7Pty8CHV7RDwui-s7O7tuTpJDwbDSu-FbEcflROarqaS7KatZmcRzlFbJUEWgzA5uhpyDRChtxwhWWDAye-C-PjxB9rlSgQpL-EvSm2E/4ns/NjIvH4O2T-uDYT-zQZn0cw/h1/h001.X0_RrAcBR32wutMLY6IaEtbMFmOrLxpzroxvTe27LJ4) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, split evenly between Analise Ortiz and Rashida Tlaib._ | | | [Chip in $15 now](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJUNGGCt2LXO_wl_jAVvqUvhIHpNSuNiKTWpEIkeAzgAO1jUqLntpvRGNGGItiHyZQPUzrveBnKg4Wk46DRqg9nE5v2ngrjdRkSaJKuyTzO1H280iA2mv0itFIC4nCIxunuoV-xlIM5kjDRgH1X45-QbYmygP2uxCeEYsmRSpvtl8XSJcUXw3exNkrAIoFnbVlXlXutJLUE8b5Z1DdwacSeAAizpfUHgSbepylABr5QjWu34K7erFXnNT91mQ8amGBFTn2omd8_mq1g_tpgquMdQjfRSrNtq4DPiqJrfzCBDZJNRPWKnlLJ943JD7pMnP4qzLtMSHeY_W6zSKamkqYvYkdm_3WPhLh6rc-W97TXkY/4ns/NjIvH4O2T-uDYT-zQZn0cw/h2/h001.WqakHu8jPooZSzl9DdwYwi0Yk9YJbmXq2hSt--pJ2d4)

At the state and federal level, MAGA Republicans are seeking to silence dissent—trampling on our rights of free speech and due process. This fascist regime is using the power of the federal government to attack our communities and our constitutional rights. I’ll keep fighting back in Congress, demanding my colleagues abolish ICE and stop ICE from murdering our neighbors. And even as Republicans continue to vilify immigrants and our movement to abolish ICE, leaders are stepping forward to counter them. Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes has stood firmly by Sen. Ortiz’s actions, stating that the Senator was clearly within her rights and stopping a county-level attorney from investigating Sen. Ortiz. We will not stay on the sidelines as these lawless immigration agencies terrorize our communities, making us unsafe in our own neighborhoods. Across the country, we’re seeing unrelenting acts of defiance against this administration. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, split evenly between Analise Ortiz and Rashida Tlaib. | | | | | | | | | We will not be silent in the face of ICE terror or other state-sanctioned violence. name: Josh Shapiro
subject: Some good news:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHAPIRO FOR PENNSYLVANIA
body:
At a moment when so many eyes were on our campaign, you stepped up

Derek, I wanted to send you this quick note to say thank you.

I know my team and I were reaching out a lot ahead of our first End-of-Month fundraising deadline of the reelection campaign. And I understand that fundraising emails like these, frankly, aren’t always a joy to see in your inbox.

But I wanted to use this opportunity to share some good news:

**Because of the generosity of so many supporters like you, we hit our January goal just a few minutes before midnight.**

At a moment when so many eyes were on our campaign — searching high and low for any signs of weakness within Team Shapiro — we didn’t give them a thing. And that’s all thanks to you.

But Derek, we can’t get ahead of ourselves. Just because we have the momentum in February... doesn’t guarantee we’ll keep it until November. We have to keep working. We have to keep fighting. We need to keep up the good work — and yes, that includes keeping our grassroots fundraising in a good spot.

**[Listen, if you chipped in ahead of our January fundraising deadline, thank you. But if you didn’t get the chance to donate — and you’re in a position to give now — I’d be extremely grateful to have your support. Because this election is just getting started, and we need to keep our foot on the gas to win it.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124511363/598843395/-1395620021?refcode=email_020126_ty&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NTY5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OWQxMTVhMy04ZGZmLWYwMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sPGqvtciL5xCg-2Yi79ZphxkEoBzUd85qCu4JlR7eeQ=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124511364/598843396/-1395620021?&amount=5&amounts=5,15,25,50,100&express_lane=true&refcode=email_020126_ty&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NTY5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OWQxMTVhMy04ZGZmLWYwMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sPGqvtciL5xCg-2Yi79ZphxkEoBzUd85qCu4JlR7eeQ=)

name: Stasha Rhodes, United for Democracy
subject: This is how we got here
disclaimer_text: Paid for by United for Democracy
body:
ICE’s actions in Minnesota are exactly why we need court reform now. | |
---
Peter, ICE has now killed two people in Minneapolis. Last year was the deadliest in decades for people in ICE custody. And a whistleblower has exposed that ICE now believes it can enter homes without a judicial warrant. **We’re supposed to be the greatest democracy in history. How on earth did we get here?** The answer, as with so much of the horrible news we face today, lies with the Supreme Court. There are two truly mind-boggling legal concepts that have been adopted in the modern court era that allow rampant abuse by federal officers—qualified immunity and the warping of what’s known as the _Bivens_ doctrine. **The idea of “qualified immunity” was introduced by the Supreme Court in 1967 to protect officers who illegally arrested Civil Rights protesters.** It’s essentially a made-up concept, wholly invented by the Court, that says federal law enforcement officials cannot be sued for violating the Constitution unless there’s a previous case showing that establishes that their actions were unconstitutional. That means that unless a court case exists deeming it unconstitutional for a federal officer to shoot an unarmed person against orders, for example, or pillage a home during a search, **they can’t be held liable for civil damages at all.** Similarly, the _Bivens_ doctrine allows people to sue state and local officials for violating the Constitution— _but not federal officers_. Until the 1980s the Supreme Court had nonetheless applied the doctrine to federal officials. **Then it stopped—and said the Court no longer recognizes a right to sue federal officials.** The ultimate irony is that federal law officers wouldn’t need protection from either qualified immunity or the _Bivens_ doctrine if they weren’t violating the Constitution. **These deliberate choices by the Supreme Court in the past have added up to the lawless, reckless federal law enforcement system we are experiencing today—from Ferguson, to George Floyd, to ICE in Minneapolis.** Today’s Court, led by Chief Justice John Roberts, Justice Brett Kavanaugh, and the rest of the MAGA majority has only exacerbated this problem. In September 2025, the Court issued a ruling over their shadow docket allowing roving ICE patrols to continue throughout the country, giving affirmative permission to ICE to racially profile and stop people based on race, ethnicity or _perceived_ ethnicity, language spoken, or even based on where they worked. Since then, ICE has been emboldened in their campaign of terror -- in social media videos, agents can be heard explicitly admitting they’ve stopped someone because they have an accent, despite the fact that [the person they’ve stopped is a U.S. Citizen.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMVZiCw9e1CjoAxuXDVyOgvAja21PRgdDEJ3ybEK8g6hzqF2_oG29qPLrofe6qxEV8WHEShHz8XJbjrNOVtj0E7JeW5sHe3zW6pg9kcPBrzgfqq_HhraIwBhMUOC8LJx8GltBhg97--MJ08p4hwsYjhc-F0JdWWlNka32z6tjha4PNVit7eodjWFgMoblJmJT_cupHMo7BKbe3bn2t1tw6NvMuz6m_JXACfRfLMww5U7TWEhEnUD9gzyJGhtjkvoG6ZpgARHUYJ2VOJc6L9efbJs/4ns/22eq7nsoTbKnODOmLoRspw/h0/h001.DXjnQkecGDpz4bnU8VpqrgvLrAqLTHzg81uMXVzmB7g) This ruling has given ICE formal permission to racially profile people, and it has led to more violence against immigrants and citizens alike. The Supreme Court’s job is to protect the rights of the people, not make it harder to sue when our rights are violated. **This is exactly why United for Democracy is fighting so hard for needed, vital court reform.** [**The Constitution should mean the same thing for everyone, not be made completely toothless for federal officers like ICE. We need a Supreme Court and justice system that protects our rights and our Constitution. Chip in today to fund the fight for court reform now!**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsQNarR6PCR1lUtXpWTyhe6ubl1AH_5uHknpsfNKSUUGJT7LD554njc9Dv6Cm_lp05RogTTmPfJS1upN4-Tt657-Rujrq7LMKpBijJdSm-TDU1t4dMtJvWC4fsuZN8Ae4wE20epDX-JqUYbx5G5XRSGwTgABUSxERFT1AoK93uj5BRK00k72C8X8lTxLZdyLEEr5y-iPgLd1KNjstr8PAUF5jwOgX0vixe4UOC21BnAfTacdwg4d1hvCyKMRXa2f1besw6YMH3H2bY_0BsQCLTAGWD56tCA1I56p4NHPGE6vH77MmAgo9G_-vugJEpdjBQ/4ns/22eq7nsoTbKnODOmLoRspw/h1/h001._s5gmBJy8Nb31Y7auydCWIJBK3XFZLsPPqS9s01umas) Thank you for fighting back against ICE’s unconstitutional lawlessness, Stasha Rhodes
United for Democracy

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw

Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

When I travel across Montana, I hear how our politicians are making life more expensive for our working families - from rising living costs to inaccessible healthcare – and **I know we can do better.**

Montana should not be run by billionaires who don’t care about us. That’s why I’m running this year. But here’s the harsh truth: winning won’t be easy, but we can’t let wealthy Republicans cling to their Senate and House majorities while everyday Americans are not being represented. [So I’m reaching out to ask if you can donate $7 or any amount that works for you, so that my campaign can continue to spread our message and secure a win this November? Politicians like Daines are running to support billionaires, not Montana’s farmers, ranchers, or economy. **Our government should be working for YOU, not billionaires.**

I’m running to give Montanans a voice at the table, stand up for our democracy, and to get our country back on track.

name: Beth Van Duyne
subject: 🚨 House Majority Alert
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETH VAN DUYNE FOR CONGRESS
body:
They only need a handful to flip control of Congress, and they’re building their entire strategy around that reality. |
---
[](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=f987a16f617f4373845542e9a226da96&_e=hpTTItfxYzPtHoyY7B1zIjAw1nZo9d2EKtADW-AZpZzcMmOk1Ss0Xwac6fVkxLsdf9_xlNRWXjlgj9Gw_70c5OQeZmxOujdqj140Ur4Arbk4yCoPlGkZfhHqIRN80jZUVSz6zTWDy0jM0GSpC4M5he5OzVpOL_14o8SeUUTftxKv3JTKJE8DipBA-8ak-q2ieOXbde5X68AnzYFCg1h8vaMMbYS5jEHJCoG6TgD6RjGau9S1aW3d8ODzw74JtGM6f__Ff5ok6YodbxPfYRv5aRozpLGOSWtTS2ZGz6Ke2rSqzLsj0710axjduaS9K-0YeqaQFqQOYfRT0F2UMhoXDsG10h1t3L8YUR4eH57qX1aYc6hqpCI6E_rtY7qHX0KygHz4PwcHiUN1Azc4iGojg-nP9hMZOSA4IOAOzL3RJOm6HvUuFV7JMYteD8KfGyYnVRZuOJpGEcBh2DpXAYxpcgeEYbFT2Vhu17ftpEr0yRaP46QivtNsKPEPxbzeGfW0kh3pC_UI9dqMEZnsD_LNIKJzyk81TwTW2VxeuOd1c--cXsqrxSZGTJt1juRQciA1enEdWzi21bZOqS2pw3ab3dIbi0EiJG1_MbUUCyHSUYZnwWdC-7kPdY7zvw4awCT9bZ7NqAKd1txbZzEe7sztLsUZRMLiYLXTz4G2jHeM-hdWLNpiVKETxsjpTyYmVkWK6vO_Iyailiu73dRvLBXB7m8t8Ogreypur-GGsEbPBhHi7rI8GA7NMPeapa6HRjQq)
Peter —

I want to be completely candid with you about what we’re walking into this year. The 2026 midterms are not routine — they’re shaping up to be a referendum election, and Democrats are already operating like they can taste the Speaker’s gavel again. Republicans hold the House right now, but our majority is razor-thin, and we cannot afford even a small slip in turnout, enthusiasm, or resources.

The hard truth is Democrats don’t need a “wave.” **They don’t need 30 seats. They only need a handful to flip control of Congress, and they’re building their entire strategy around that reality.** They’re targeting competitive districts nationwide, recruiting challengers, and using every headline they can to fuel small-dollar fundraising and motivate their base.

And we all know what happens if Democrats take the House. Deporting criminal illegal aliens is shutdown. We’ll be fighting tax increases instead of delivering tax cuts. And crazy Leftists will run all the committees. **Instead of results, Washington becomes a circus again — investigations, subpoenas, political stunts, and endless obstruction designed to paralyze President Trump’s final two years in office.** If Democrats flip the House, the America First agenda is dead on arrival.

That’s why I’m reaching out today. We have a narrow window to build the resources we need early so we can define this race on our terms, win the air war, and fund a serious turnout operation before Democrats’ outside money starts flooding in.

clean_body: They only need a handful to flip control of Congress, and they’re building their entire strategy around that reality. | Peter — I want to be completely candid with you about what we’re walking into this year. The 2026 midterms are not routine — they’re shaping up to be a referendum election, and Democrats are already operating like they can taste the Speaker’s gavel again. Republicans hold the House right now, but our majority is razor-thin, and we cannot afford even a small slip in turnout, enthusiasm, or resources. The hard truth is Democrats don’t need a “wave.” They don’t need 30 seats. They only need a handful to flip control of Congress, and they’re building their entire strategy around that reality. They’re targeting competitive districts nationwide, recruiting challengers, and using every headline they can to fuel small-dollar fundraising and motivate their base. And we all know what happens if Democrats take the House. Deporting criminal illegal aliens is shutdown. We’ll be fighting tax increases instead of delivering tax cuts. And crazy Leftists will run all the committees. Instead of results, Washington becomes a circus again — investigations, subpoenas, political stunts, and endless obstruction designed to paralyze President Trump’s final two years in office. If Democrats flip the House, the America First agenda is dead on arrival. That’s why I’m reaching out today. We have a narrow window to build the resources we need early so we can define this race on our terms, win the air war, and fund a serious turnout operation before Democrats’ outside money starts flooding in. We’ve worked too hard to let the radical left claw their way back into power and turn Congress into an emergency brake on President Trump’s agenda, Peter. Thank you, Beth Van Duyne | | PAID FOR BY BETH VAN DUYNE FOR CONGRESS

The White House confirmed that they’re drafting a new executive order to restrict Americans' right to vote by mail.

_Despite Trump’s efforts to sow distrust in our electoral system, here’s what we know to be _actually_ true:_

–Nearly a third of Americans used mail-in voting to cast their ballots in the 2024 election

–Experts say that easier access to ballots leads to higher turnout in our elections

–Millions of mail-in ballots have been cast safely over the years!
