clean_body: The work we are doing in the Senate is too important to stop - but our majority is in grave peril. | Fellow Conservative, I’m not writing you today to alarm you, but rather to help you understand how precarious our current situation is. During the 2024 campaign, your hard-earned dollars propelled us to victory, capturing not only the House, but the Senate and the White House. And I believe that the last twelve months have been among the most productive in Washington in DECADES. But if polls are to be believed, then that productivity, our majority, and perhaps even the future of our Republic as we know it today are in danger. There is no way to put this other than to deliver the bad news outright: Democrats have extended their lead over Republicans by 14 points on the generic ballot. If you’ll recall, I only won my seat in Montana by a little more than 7.5%, and in Texas, my friend Sen. Ted Cruz won his race by just over 9%. Hate is a powerful motivator, and Democrats abhor President Trump and all those who stand alongside him. But there is still time. No more government shrinkage. No more tax cuts. No more border enforcement measures. I didn’t fight every single day of 2023 and 2024 to win a Senate seat against the most endangered Senate Democrat, only to watch us lose our precious majority because Democrats motivate their voters through lies and hatred of everything we’re doing to make America a better place for everyone. For America's Future, Tim Sheehy U.S. Senator - Montana | " "I SERVED AS A NAVY SEAL, BUILT ONE OF THE LARGEST AERIAL FIREFIGHTING COMPANIES IN AMERICA CREATING OVER 200 MONTANA JOBS, AND STARTED A VETERAN-LED CATTLE RANCHING AND FEEDING OPERATION. WHETHER IT WAS IN WAR OR BUSINESS, I SEE PROBLEMS AND SOLVE THEM. AMERICA NEEDS CONSERVATIVE LEADERS WHO LOVE OUR COUNTRY. I'M RUNNING FOR THE U.S. SENATE BECAUSE OUR CAMPAIGN IS ABOUT SERVICE, DUTY, AND COUNTRY -- NOT POLITICS AS USUAL." | PAID FOR BY TIM SHEEHY FOR MONTANA Tim Sheehy is a former Navy SEAL. We’ve elected our first congressional candidate of 2026.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260201-1&t=1&akid=3108%2E587540%2EG2R0dX)
**WE WON, Peter!**
**Christian Menefee just clinched victory in Texas’ 18th Congressional District.**
This is a powerful win for our movement. Christian is now officially the first congressional candidate we’ve elected in 2026 — and we aren’t even thinking about slowing down.
**[Will you donate $5 to help us continue this momentum into the midterms?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260201_1_houston.wtC1-M&refcode=em20260201-3108&amount=5&t=2&akid=3108%2E587540%2EG2R0dX)**
Because of your grassroots support, we were able to elect a real fighter with a record of standing up for his community, including taking the Trump administration to court (and winning), holding polluters accountable, and protecting families from price gouging.
And with people like you behind him, Christian was able to pull off this win without selling out to corporate PACs and billionaires. He won this race _because_ of his grassroots campaign.
We’re bringing this model to all of the campaigns we’re focusing on this year. Together, we are going to elect Donavan McKinney, Lauren Babb Tomlinson, Patrick Roath, Justin J. Pearson, and every one of our endorsed candidates _without a dime_ from corporate PACs.
It’s only possible with your continued support, Peter. Will you stay with us in the fight?
**[Pitch in $5 or anything you can right now to back our movement in 2026.](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260201_1_houston.wtC1-M&refcode=em20260201-3108&amount=5&t=3&akid=3108%2E587540%2EG2R0dX)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260201_1_houston.wtC1-M&amount=5&refcode=em20260201-3108&t=22&akid=3108%2E587540%2EG2R0dX)**
Let’s keep winning,
David Hogg

name: Haley Stevens
subject: I need to know what you think
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
I’m calling for the impeachment of secretary of Homeland Security, Kristi Noem

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=2115%2E49239%2EpnyCbq)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/13550?t=2&akid=2115%2E49239%2EpnyCbq)
---
Null, it's Haley. Please read:

**I’m calling for the impeachment of Kristi Noem as secretary of Homeland Security.**
This is a critical moment for our country, and we need political courage from both Republicans and Democrats.
I was left with no choice after Secretary Noem ignored my repeated calls to open investigations into the murder of Renee Nicole Good in Minneapolis, the death of Nenko Gantchev, a detainee at the North Lake Processing Center in Baldwin, Michigan, and, most recently, the killing of Alex Pretti late last month.

Over the last year, Secretary Noem’s leadership has been reckless, chaotic, and corrupt. Under her purview, our country is less safe. It’s time for accountability.
In moments like this, it means a lot to have you with me, null. Please respond to my live poll now to let me know if you agree:
**DO YOU APPROVE OF KRISTI NOEM?**
| | | [YES](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/13551?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=3&akid=2115%2E49239%2EpnyCbq)
---
| [NO](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/13551?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=4&akid=2115%2E49239%2EpnyCbq)
---
Thank you,

Haley Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/13552?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=5&akid=2115%2E49239%2EpnyCbq) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/13550?t=6&akid=2115%2E49239%2EpnyCbq)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=7&akid=2115%2E49239%2EpnyCbq)

33717 Woodward Ave #539
Birmingham, MI 48009

name: Laila Mohib, Emerge
subject: Fwd: [Invitation] This Moment Demands Leadership — Join Us Thursday
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emerge
body:
Hi Peter,
Bumping this to the top of your inbox to make sure you got your invitation to our upcoming #LeadersMatter event this Thursday , featuring Emerge alums US Representative LaMonica McIver, CA State Senator Sasha Renée Pérez, and Maine State Representative Deqa Dhalac . RSVP today [https://secure.ngpvan.com/6dpCtmE-0E2XmNTocrlsng2] to secure your spot and hear from these inspiring women about their experiences on the frontlines fighting ICE.
Warm regards,
Laila Mohib
Senior Advisor, Office of the President
emergeamerica.org [emergeamerica.org]
Dear Peter,
We’re living through a moment that demands clarity and courage.
What’s happening in Minnesota—and across the country—has made the consequences of immigration enforcement impossible to ignore. Communities are experiencing fear and harm in real time, and leadership is being tested.
Join Emerge for a timely virtual conversation with three of our alums, Congresswoman LaMonica McIver, California State Senator Sasha Renée Pérez, and Maine State Representative Deqa Dhalac on what they’re seeing on the ground and how they’re leading in this moment.
Thursday, February 5
5pm ET / 2pm PT
RSVP HERE [https://secure.ngpvan.com/6dpCtmE-0E2XmNTocrlsng2]
For more than 20 years, Emerge has trained women to run for office not just to win—but to lead when it matters most. This conversation is part of that commitment.
We hope you’ll join us.
https://secure.ngpvan.com/6dpCtmE-0E2XmNTocrlsng2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/6dpCtmE-0E2XmNTocrlsng2]
In solidarity,
Team Emerge

* **TOP NEWS**

**" Texas stunner: Democrat Taylor Rehmet flips Republican state Senate district Trump won by 17 points" **AP's **John Hanna** and**Julie Carr Smyth** - Democrat**Taylor Rehmet** flipped a reliably Republican state Senate district in Texas in Saturday's special election, continuing a string of surprise victories for Democrats across the U.S. in the year since**Donald Trump** returned to the White House.

The Republican president immediately distanced himself from the loss. It's a district he had won by 17 points in 2024.

At Mar-a-Lago on Sunday, Trump told reporters, "I'm not involved in that. That's a local Texas race."

Just a day earlier, however, he had used his social media platform to urge voters to support the Republican candidate, conservative activist **Leigh Wambsganns**. He called her a successful entrepreneur and "an incredible supporter" of his Make America Great Again movement.

Rehmet, a labor union leader and veteran, defeated Wambsganss easily in the Fort Worth-area district. With almost all votes counted, Rehmet was leading by more than 14 percentage points -- a more than 30-point difference.

"This win goes to everyday working people," Rehmet told supporters.

Republican Texas Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** called it "a wake-up call for Republicans across Texas," where the GOP controls every statewide office.

"Our voters cannot take anything for granted," Patrick wrote on the social platform X, while noting low-turnout special elections are always unpredictable. "I know the energy and strength the Republican grassroots in Texas possess. We will come out fighting with a new resolve, and we will take this seat back in November."

Rehmet's victory added to Democrats' record of overperforming in special elections so far this cycle, beginning in March -- when they prevailed in a Pennsylvania legislative district made up of suburbanites and farmers that [Democrats hadn't held in a century](https://substack.com/redirect/3209d3ef-1b1e-4f0e-9d80-bed1679fab9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- and continuing through to November, when they [dominated candidate and ballot contests](https://substack.com/redirect/ae665a8e-2875-4a0b-8956-cdcdbc13c17b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from Maine to California. ...

Democrats said Saturday's results in Texas were further evidence that voters under the second Trump administration are motivated to reject GOP candidates and their policies.

Texas Democratic Party Chair **Kendall Scudder** said Rehmet won by standing with working people and talking to Texans about the future.

"This win shows what is possible in Texas with strong organizing, great candidates and strategic investments," he said in a statement. "People are noticing that Democrats have the workers' backs and are delivering results."" [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/fc3ed646-53c0-49bd-8abe-158200ee2326?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**" Deadly, drug-resistant fungus spreading in Texas. Here's who is at risk"** via [Fort Worth Star-Telegram](https://substack.com/redirect/80272b33-7dbd-436c-8739-0d3c79730a3d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) - A deadly, drug-resistant fungus has infected more than 27 states in the last year, including Texas.

It's called Candida auris. Since its emergence in the U.S. in 2016, the fungus has had a 34 percent mortality rate between 2016 and 2022, according to data from the Center for Disease Control, which now considers C. auris as a notifiable infectious disease.

As of Dec. 27, 2025 Texas reported 830 clinical cases of the fungal infection for that year.

Here's what to know about Candida auris in Texas.

Candida auris is a yeast infection common in sick patients at healthcare facilities.
C. auris can cause both skin or bloodstream infections. The fungus commonly enters the body through pathways via medical devices like catheters, breathing or feeding tubes.

What makes it drug resistant is its ability to resist antifungal medications.

"The fungus develops the ability to defeat the drugs designed to kill it," according to the CDC. "That means the germs are not killed and continue to grow."

**" Gov. Greg Abbott says ICE needs to 'recalibrate' mission after deadly Minnesota shooting"** via [Dallas Morning News](https://substack.com/redirect/6475e64c-f2ee-4ad4-9028-64ea63cd334c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) - Gov. **Greg Abbott** said Monday the White House needs to "recalibrate" Immigration and Customs Enforcement's mission to rebuild public trust after federal agents fatally shot a second protester in Minnesota.

Abbott said immigration authorities should "get back to what they wanted to do to begin with" and focus on removing "people from the country who are here illegally."

He said respect for law enforcement, including ICE, is essential and that the Trump administration needs "to recalibrate on what needs to be done" to restore that respect, "especially under the current circumstances."

On conservative radio host **Mark Davis** ' Dallas-based show, Abbott said he believes federal officials are working on a "game plan" for agents to carry out removals "in a more structured way," without causing "all the kinds of problems and fighting in communities" seen now.

(_[DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/ac1b7145-47b4-4849-b56d-812d82c4d5d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Abbott Speaks on State Examining H-1B Visas in Schools, Violence in Minnesota"** [The Texan's](https://substack.com/redirect/4ad1e708-721b-40da-b7f9-72f96d72ec28?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Mary Elise O 'Bar** - **Greg Abbott** confirmed during a Monday morning radio spot that his office is collecting data on H-1B visas in Texas, particularly in the state's public schools, and also commented on the recent fatal shooting of a man in Minneapolis, Minnesota by a federal immigration agent -- emphasizing Texas' cooperation with law enforcement.

Internal communications published by Quorum Report on Saturday indicated that Abbott was examining H-1B visas in the state's school system via emails sent to leaders of the Texas A&M University System, who were instructed to compile a list of employees on H-1B visas and report back by Monday.

(_[THE TEXAN](https://substack.com/redirect/54cef9c1-a888-4a12-b81e-3f8b08ef8c1b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Abbott widens winter storm disaster to cover 219 Texas counties"** [Midland Reporter-Telegram's](https://substack.com/redirect/941f6497-282c-4e12-9613-c5c15b2f4e3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **B. Kay Richter** - AUSTIN -- **Greg Abbott** has amended the state's original disaster declaration for Winter Storm Fern to include 85 additional counties facing impacts from winter weather conditions.

Within the original disaster declaration issued on Thursday, Jan. 22, counties across the Permian Basin, including Midland and Ector, were listed. Additionally, surrounding counties such as Andrews, Borden, Crane, Dawson, Gaines, Glasscock, Howard, Loving, Martin, Reagan, Reeves, Sterling, Upton, Ward and Winkler were part of the original disaster decree. During the weekend, Abbott amended the state's original disaster declaration for the ongoing winter storm to include Pecos County and others.

"Communities across the state have been impacted by the weekend's winter weather," Abbott said in a statement. "I am updating my disaster declaration to include additional counties to help more Texans during these freezing temperatures. Texans should continue to monitor local weather forecasts and road conditions to keep themselves and their families safe."

In all, the disaster declaration includes 219 counties.

* **2026**

**" Top Hunt aide charged taxpayers $44,000 in hotel expenses" **[Politico's](https://substack.com/redirect/1c4e0bd9-d316-45ae-b1bb-bbce358edf0e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**Daniel Lippman** - The travel expenses of a Republican campaign manager in the crowded Texas Senate race have raised eyebrows for their seemingly exorbitant amount.

**James Kyrkanides** , the campaign manager for Senate candidate **Wesley Hunt,** charged taxpayers more than $44,000 in hotel expenses over three years as Hunt's congressional chief of staff. The charges average $457 per night, four times the [national standard rate set by General Services Administration rate](https://substack.com/redirect/02e5e7bc-62c8-4a4e-9d23-14b30db45f6f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for many federal employees, according to a POLITICO analysis of House expense records.

That total accrued over 97 nights during 49 different stays. For instance, on Jan. 3, 2025, when Congress was being sworn in, Kyrkanides expensed a one-night hotel bill of $805. For May 20 to 22, 2024, he submitted a hotel bill of $1,692. And for a stay starting Aug. 6, 2024, he expensed two nights for a total of $1,321.

The expense records do not require the hotels or the city in which they're located to be listed, and Kyrkanides declined to name the hotels.

Kyrkanides said all of the expenses were reviewed and approved by the House finance office, which handles disbursements of member money. He said less than half of the money that was reimbursed went to him personally and the remainder covered travel expenses for other Hunt staffers. He declined to provide receipts to verify that claim.

Kyrkanides confirmed Washington was his primary workplace during that period of time. He said a number of the reimbursements were in Hunt's hometown of Houston, where the GSA's [recommended federal lodging rate](https://substack.com/redirect/460af169-d1f6-4f45-8892-628f8f2a688f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is $128 per night. Krykanides said expenses also included stays in Washington, San Diego and New York, which have had average GSA rates of $234, $205 and $277 over the last year, respectively. Krykanides said the hotel stays in those cities were expensed on behalf of Hunt staffers for work travel, including a district director conference in San Diego and communications staffers going to New York for Fox News interviews related to policy.

Kyrkanides also noted that costs of food, beverage and parking aren't included in the GSA rates, but his reimbursements folded in those items. He said subtracting them brings his average nightly lodging costs, excluding taxes, to $269. He also pointed to GSA policy that allows federal employees to be reimbursed up to 300 percent of the recommended rate if [no other rooms](https://substack.com/redirect/56848af7-ebfb-40dd-bdf6-d6d77a041a9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are available.

Asked for comment about the expenses, a Hunt spokesperson said in a statement: "All transactions in this office are approved by the chief administrative officer, made public, and carried out in accordance with the highest ethical guidelines."" [Politico](https://substack.com/redirect/1c4e0bd9-d316-45ae-b1bb-bbce358edf0e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**" Sen. Ted Cruz pitches 2026 Texas Senate race while steering most donations to his campaign" **Dallas Morning News'**Joseph Morton** - It starts as an [urgent warning](https://substack.com/redirect/bb15bbec-c331-4174-a5c3-26d9db725108?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

Texas' primary is speeding closer, Sen. **Ted Cruz** says, the nation is watching and Republicans need to get off the sidelines to win in 2026.

Later come buttons to make a donation, a familiar step in fundraising emails. The pitch reassures your money will help both Cruz and the future Republican nominee for the U.S. Senate in Texas, a two-for-one play to counter Democrats.

If you stop to click on a small-type link for additional details, the math appears. With a $100 donation, $1 is dedicated to a new fund for whoever wins the GOP nomination.

The rest, $99, goes to Cruz's campaign account, unless donors change the allocation.

Cruz's recent appeal to conservative donors is a staple of modern campaign fundraising emails, with preset donation amounts, flashing buttons and fine print dividing contributions.

The approach is legal and reflects the growing prevalence of aggressive political email appeals that critics say are often manipulative.

Cruz's effort has stirred questions in Washington, with early donations meant to fully help the nominee largely being steered to Cruz.

Some election law experts called it deceptive, trading on concerns about the Texas Senate race to benefit Cruz, who is not on the 2026 ballot but has been mentioned as a presidential contender in two years.

Attention over campaign money is even sharper this cycle because of the high-profile, [high-spending Republican primary](https://substack.com/redirect/95ec3c2c-6174-46f4-bda5-0a0b576e0881?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) among Sen.**John Cornyn** , Attorney General **Ken Paxton** and U.S. Rep. **Wesley Hunt** of Houston.

"Cruz is basically diverting money away from the Cornyn-Paxton race for his own personal ambition," said campaign finance lawyer **Brett Kappel** , a former legal counsel to three presidential campaigns.

Cruz spokesperson **Macarena Martinez** dismissed that, saying his outreach is aimed at keeping Texas Republican and ensuring a GOP victory in November.

"No Republican in the country has devoted more time and spent more money to elect Republican senators and House members than Sen. Cruz," she said.

She said any criticism of Cruz's appeal "conveniently ignores the well-known fact that this standard split is routinely used by both Democrats and Republicans when a campaign pays the cost of a digital solicitation."

The Cornyn, Paxton and Hunt campaigns declined to comment on Cruz's fundraising approach."

(_[DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/dddf2045-e685-41ba-bbd5-93462d45de92?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Heated GOP Primary for Southeast Texas House District Draws Scrutiny Over Public School Education Foundation Spending"** [The Texan's](https://substack.com/redirect/8e7e1450-014e-46c8-b960-f71bbb2319d6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Holly Hansen** - A heated Republican battle is emerging in southeast Texas, where state Rep. **Terri Leo Wilson** (R-Galveston) faces a primary challenge from a Chambers County developer in a race shaped by conflicting characterizations of Leo Wilson's voting record -- and renewed scrutiny of a tiny school district's relationship with a lucrative non-profit public-school foundation.

Former Mont Belvieu City Manager **Nathan Watkins** , vice president at Americus Holdings Real Estate Development and a director of the Barbers Hill Independent School District's (BHISD) education foundation, announced last year that he would run against Leo Wilson for Texas House District 23, which includes Chambers County and part of Galveston County.

(_[THE TEXAN](https://substack.com/redirect/05eadef3-d921-450e-bf45-a0bddadbb2ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)
* * *
* **TEXANS IN DC**

**" Rep. Julie Johnson traded stocks from company that helps Trump's deportation efforts despite anti-ICE comments"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/304305cf-8b8d-459b-82bc-9025b0b6b708?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Olivia Borgula** - **Julie Johnson** , a Farmers Branch Democrat who was elected in 2024 to represent Texas' 32rd Congressional District, bought and sold stocks from Palantir Technologies, a contractor with the federal government that has been key to the Trump administration's efforts to track and deport undocumented immigrants.

Johnson bought Palantir stock days before President**Donald Trump** took office for his second term on Jan. 15, 2025, and again on Feb. 12, 2025, according to congressional financial disclosure reports. She sold her shares a few months later in April and June of 2025, the reports show, and for each sale she reported earning between $1,001 and $15,000. In a statement to the Texas Tribune, Johnson said the stocks were managed through independent third parties and that she started divesting her holdings in March, and that all Palantir holdings were sold by June.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/e76fe3fb-a56e-49bd-b518-2fcc0475f1a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . TOP NEWS " Texas stunner: Democrat Taylor Rehmet flips Republican state Senate district Trump won by 17 points" AP's John Hanna andJulie Carr Smyth - DemocratTaylor Rehmet flipped a reliably Republican state Senate district in Texas in Saturday's special election, continuing a string of surprise victories for Democrats across the U.S. in the year sinceDonald Trump returned to the White House. The Republican president immediately distanced himself from the loss. It's a district he had won by 17 points in 2024. At Mar-a-Lago on Sunday, Trump told reporters, "I'm not involved in that. That's a local Texas race." Just a day earlier, however, he had used his social media platform to urge voters to support the Republican candidate, conservative activist Leigh Wambsganns. He called her a successful entrepreneur and "an incredible supporter" of his Make America Great Again movement. Rehmet, a labor union leader and veteran, defeated Wambsganss easily in the Fort Worth-area district. With almost all votes counted, Rehmet was leading by more than 14 percentage points -- a more than 30-point difference. "This win goes to everyday working people," Rehmet told supporters. Republican Texas Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick called it "a wake-up call for Republicans across Texas," where the GOP controls every statewide office. "Our voters cannot take anything for granted," Patrick wrote on the social platform X, while noting low-turnout special elections are always unpredictable. "I know the energy and strength the Republican grassroots in Texas possess. We will come out fighting with a new resolve, and we will take this seat back in November." Rehmet's victory added to Democrats' record of overperforming in special elections so far this cycle, beginning in March -- when they prevailed in a Pennsylvania legislative district made up of suburbanites and farmers that -- and continuing through to November, when they from Maine to California. ... Democrats said Saturday's results in Texas were further evidence that voters under the second Trump administration are motivated to reject GOP candidates and their policies. Texas Democratic Party Chair Kendall Scudder said Rehmet won by standing with working people and talking to Texans about the future. "This win shows what is possible in Texas with strong organizing, great candidates and strategic investments," he said in a statement. "People are noticing that Democrats have the workers' backs and are delivering results."" " Deadly, drug-resistant fungus spreading in Texas. Here's who is at risk" via - A deadly, drug-resistant fungus has infected more than 27 states in the last year, including Texas. It's called Candida auris. Since its emergence in the U.S. in 2016, the fungus has had a 34 percent mortality rate between 2016 and 2022, according to data from the Center for Disease Control, which now considers C. auris as a notifiable infectious disease. As of Dec. 27, 2025 Texas reported 830 clinical cases of the fungal infection for that year. Here's what to know about Candida auris in Texas. Candida auris is a yeast infection common in sick patients at healthcare facilities. C. auris can cause both skin or bloodstream infections. The fungus commonly enters the body through pathways via medical devices like catheters, breathing or feeding tubes. What makes it drug resistant is its ability to resist antifungal medications. "The fungus develops the ability to defeat the drugs designed to kill it," according to the CDC. "That means the germs are not killed and continue to grow." () STATE GOVERNMENT " Gov. Greg Abbott says ICE needs to 'recalibrate' mission after deadly Minnesota shooting" via - Gov. Greg Abbott said Monday the White House needs to "recalibrate" Immigration and Customs Enforcement's mission to rebuild public trust after federal agents fatally shot a second protester in Minnesota. Abbott said immigration authorities should "get back to what they wanted to do to begin with" and focus on removing "people from the country who are here illegally." He said respect for law enforcement, including ICE, is essential and that the Trump administration needs "to recalibrate on what needs to be done" to restore that respect, "especially under the current circumstances." On conservative radio host Mark Davis ' Dallas-based show, Abbott said he believes federal officials are working on a "game plan" for agents to carry out removals "in a more structured way," without causing "all the kinds of problems and fighting in communities" seen now. () " Abbott Speaks on State Examining H-1B Visas in Schools, Violence in Minnesota" Mary Elise O 'Bar - Greg Abbott confirmed during a Monday morning radio spot that his office is collecting data on H-1B visas in Texas, particularly in the state's public schools, and also commented on the recent fatal shooting of a man in Minneapolis, Minnesota by a federal immigration agent -- emphasizing Texas' cooperation with law enforcement. Internal communications published by Quorum Report on Saturday indicated that Abbott was examining H-1B visas in the state's school system via emails sent to leaders of the Texas A&M University System, who were instructed to compile a list of employees on H-1B visas and report back by Monday. () " Abbott widens winter storm disaster to cover 219 Texas counties" B. Kay Richter - AUSTIN -- Greg Abbott has amended the state's original disaster declaration for Winter Storm Fern to include 85 additional counties facing impacts from winter weather conditions. Within the original disaster declaration issued on Thursday, Jan. 22, counties across the Permian Basin, including Midland and Ector, were listed. Additionally, surrounding counties such as Andrews, Borden, Crane, Dawson, Gaines, Glasscock, Howard, Loving, Martin, Reagan, Reeves, Sterling, Upton, Ward and Winkler were part of the original disaster decree. During the weekend, Abbott amended the state's original disaster declaration for the ongoing winter storm to include Pecos County and others. "Communities across the state have been impacted by the weekend's winter weather," Abbott said in a statement. "I am updating my disaster declaration to include additional counties to help more Texans during these freezing temperatures. Texans should continue to monitor local weather forecasts and road conditions to keep themselves and their families safe." In all, the disaster declaration includes 219 counties. () " Paxton Confirms Comptroller Can Block ESA Funds for Schools Tied to Terrorist Groups" Meridith Dyer - Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton issued a legal opinion confirming the Texas Comptroller of Public Accounts' full authority to block taxpayer funds from going to schools tied to terrorist groups or foreign adversaries, according to a January 24 press release. On December 12, Hancock requested an opinion from Paxton asking if schools with such ties are legally disqualified from participating in the state's education savings account program, Texas Education Freedom Accounts (TEFA). () LOCAL GOVERNMENT " Dallas City Council Resolves Not to Support Elevated High-Speed Rail" Kim Roberts - The Dallas City Council passed a resolution last week that reaffirms its 2024 decision that any high-speed rail line between Dallas and Fort Worth should not be built above-ground through the Central Business District, Victory Park, nor the uptown area of the city. However, Councilmember Paul Ridley noted that the future of the high-speed rail line is now more uncertain, as last week Congress defunded $928 million in high-speed rail grants during its negotiation of a transportation appropriations bill. () " Austin Light Rail Proposal Passes Environmental Assessment in Effort for Federal Funding" Kim Roberts - The Austin Transit Partnership is celebrating that its light rail system has completed a "major milestone" required before construction can begin. The Federal Transit Administration (FTA) has issued its record of decision, finding that the project meets the environmental requirements and approving its environmental impact statement. () 2026 " Top Hunt aide charged taxpayers $44,000 in hotel expenses" Lippman - The travel expenses of a Republican campaign manager in the crowded Texas Senate race have raised eyebrows for their seemingly exorbitant amount. James Kyrkanides , the campaign manager for Senate candidate Wesley Hunt, charged taxpayers more than $44,000 in hotel expenses over three years as Hunt's congressional chief of staff. The charges average $457 per night, four times the for many federal employees, according to a POLITICO analysis of House expense records. That total accrued over 97 nights during 49 different stays. For instance, on Jan. 3, 2025, when Congress was being sworn in, Kyrkanides expensed a one-night hotel bill of $805. For May 20 to 22, 2024, he submitted a hotel bill of $1,692. And for a stay starting Aug. 6, 2024, he expensed two nights for a total of $1,321. The expense records do not require the hotels or the city in which they're located to be listed, and Kyrkanides declined to name the hotels. Kyrkanides said all of the expenses were reviewed and approved by the House finance office, which handles disbursements of member money. He said less than half of the money that was reimbursed went to him personally and the remainder covered travel expenses for other Hunt staffers. He declined to provide receipts to verify that claim. Kyrkanides confirmed Washington was his primary workplace during that period of time. He said a number of the reimbursements were in Hunt's hometown of Houston, where the GSA's is $128 per night. Krykanides said expenses also included stays in Washington, San Diego and New York, which have had average GSA rates of $234, $205 and $277 over the last year, respectively. Krykanides said the hotel stays in those cities were expensed on behalf of Hunt staffers for work travel, including a district director conference in San Diego and communications staffers going to New York for Fox News interviews related to policy. Kyrkanides also noted that costs of food, beverage and parking aren't included in the GSA rates, but his reimbursements folded in those items. He said subtracting them brings his average nightly lodging costs, excluding taxes, to $269. He also pointed to GSA policy that allows federal employees to be reimbursed up to 300 percent of the recommended rate if are available. Asked for comment about the expenses, a Hunt spokesperson said in a statement: "All transactions in this office are approved by the chief administrative officer, made public, and carried out in accordance with the highest ethical guidelines."" " Sen. Ted Cruz pitches 2026 Texas Senate race while steering most donations to his campaign" Dallas Morning News'Joseph Morton - It starts as an . Texas' primary is speeding closer, Sen. Ted Cruz says, the nation is watching and Republicans need to get off the sidelines to win in 2026. Later come buttons to make a donation, a familiar step in fundraising emails. The pitch reassures your money will help both Cruz and the future Republican nominee for the U.S. Senate in Texas, a two-for-one play to counter Democrats. If you stop to click on a small-type link for additional details, the math appears. With a $100 donation, $1 is dedicated to a new fund for whoever wins the GOP nomination. The rest, $99, goes to Cruz's campaign account, unless donors change the allocation. Cruz's recent appeal to conservative donors is a staple of modern campaign fundraising emails, with preset donation amounts, flashing buttons and fine print dividing contributions. The approach is legal and reflects the growing prevalence of aggressive political email appeals that critics say are often manipulative. Cruz's effort has stirred questions in Washington, with early donations meant to fully help the nominee largely being steered to Cruz. Some election law experts called it deceptive, trading on concerns about the Texas Senate race to benefit Cruz, who is not on the 2026 ballot but has been mentioned as a presidential contender in two years. Attention over campaign money is even sharper this cycle because of the high-profile, among Sen.John Cornyn , Attorney General Ken Paxton and U.S. Rep. Wesley Hunt of Houston. "Cruz is basically diverting money away from the Cornyn-Paxton race for his own personal ambition," said campaign finance lawyer Brett Kappel , a former legal counsel to three presidential campaigns. Cruz spokesperson Macarena Martinez dismissed that, saying his outreach is aimed at keeping Texas Republican and ensuring a GOP victory in November. "No Republican in the country has devoted more time and spent more money to elect Republican senators and House members than Sen. Cruz," she said. She said any criticism of Cruz's appeal "conveniently ignores the well-known fact that this standard split is routinely used by both Democrats and Republicans when a campaign pays the cost of a digital solicitation." The Cornyn, Paxton and Hunt campaigns declined to comment on Cruz's fundraising approach." () " Heated GOP Primary for Southeast Texas House District Draws Scrutiny Over Public School Education Foundation Spending" Holly Hansen - A heated Republican battle is emerging in southeast Texas, where state Rep. Terri Leo Wilson (R-Galveston) faces a primary challenge from a Chambers County developer in a race shaped by conflicting characterizations of Leo Wilson's voting record -- and renewed scrutiny of a tiny school district's relationship with a lucrative non-profit public-school foundation. Former Mont Belvieu City Manager Nathan Watkins , vice president at Americus Holdings Real Estate Development and a director of the Barbers Hill Independent School District's (BHISD) education foundation, announced last year that he would run against Leo Wilson for Texas House District 23, which includes Chambers County and part of Galveston County. () TEXANS IN DC " Rep. Julie Johnson traded stocks from company that helps Trump's deportation efforts despite anti-ICE comments" Olivia Borgula - Julie Johnson , a Farmers Branch Democrat who was elected in 2024 to represent Texas' 32rd Congressional District, bought and sold stocks from Palantir Technologies, a contractor with the federal government that has been key to the Trump administration's efforts to track and deport undocumented immigrants. Johnson bought Palantir stock days before PresidentDonald Trump took office for his second term on Jan. 15, 2025, and again on Feb. 12, 2025, according to congressional financial disclosure reports. She sold her shares a few months later in April and June of 2025, the reports show, and for each sale she reported earning between $1,001 and $15,000. In a statement to the Texas Tribune, Johnson said the stocks were managed through independent third parties and that she started divesting her holdings in March, and that all Palantir holdings were sold by June. * * *
* **TOP NEWS**

**" Texas stunner: Democrat Taylor Rehmet flips Republican state Senate district Trump won by 17 points" **AP's **John Hanna** and**Julie Carr Smyth** - Democrat**Taylor Rehmet** flipped a reliably Republican state Senate district in Texas in Saturday's special election, continuing a string of surprise victories for Democrats across the U.S. in the year since**Donald Trump** returned to the White House.

The Republican president immediately distanced himself from the loss. It's a district he had won by 17 points in 2024.

At Mar-a-Lago on Sunday, Trump told reporters, "I'm not involved in that. That's a local Texas race."

Just a day earlier, however, he had used his social media platform to urge voters to support the Republican candidate, conservative activist **Leigh Wambsganns**. He called her a successful entrepreneur and "an incredible supporter" of his Make America Great Again movement.

Rehmet, a labor union leader and veteran, defeated Wambsganss easily in the Fort Worth-area district. With almost all votes counted, Rehmet was leading by more than 14 percentage points -- a more than 30-point difference.

"This win goes to everyday working people," Rehmet told supporters.

Republican Texas Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** called it "a wake-up call for Republicans across Texas," where the GOP controls every statewide office.

"Our voters cannot take anything for granted," Patrick wrote on the social platform X, while noting low-turnout special elections are always unpredictable. "I know the energy and strength the Republican grassroots in Texas possess. We will come out fighting with a new resolve, and we will take this seat back in November."

Rehmet's victory added to Democrats' record of overperforming in special elections so far this cycle, beginning in March -- when they prevailed in a Pennsylvania legislative district made up of suburbanites and farmers that [Democrats hadn't held in a century](https://substack.com/redirect/25ed9267-6b08-4d45-a667-dd201aef8f9c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- and continuing through to November, when they [dominated candidate and ballot contests](https://substack.com/redirect/b5ba4c9e-15b2-4af3-bf4b-64b7f149d410?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from Maine to California. ...

Democrats said Saturday's results in Texas were further evidence that voters under the second Trump administration are motivated to reject GOP candidates and their policies.

Texas Democratic Party Chair **Kendall Scudder** said Rehmet won by standing with working people and talking to Texans about the future.

"This win shows what is possible in Texas with strong organizing, great candidates and strategic investments," he said in a statement. "People are noticing that Democrats have the workers' backs and are delivering results."" [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/c1b6420f-47fd-4b6d-85ec-6d9548ff5be2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**" Deadly, drug-resistant fungus spreading in Texas. Here's who is at risk"** via [Fort Worth Star-Telegram](https://substack.com/redirect/6991ae24-acad-4a32-9edc-4f983abf0e27?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) - A deadly, drug-resistant fungus has infected more than 27 states in the last year, including Texas.

It's called Candida auris. Since its emergence in the U.S. in 2016, the fungus has had a 34 percent mortality rate between 2016 and 2022, according to data from the Center for Disease Control, which now considers C. auris as a notifiable infectious disease.

As of Dec. 27, 2025 Texas reported 830 clinical cases of the fungal infection for that year.

Here's what to know about Candida auris in Texas.

Candida auris is a yeast infection common in sick patients at healthcare facilities.
C. auris can cause both skin or bloodstream infections. The fungus commonly enters the body through pathways via medical devices like catheters, breathing or feeding tubes.

What makes it drug resistant is its ability to resist antifungal medications.

"The fungus develops the ability to defeat the drugs designed to kill it," according to the CDC. "That means the germs are not killed and continue to grow."

(_[FWST](https://substack.com/redirect/67bf2803-a7a7-4bc4-8fe6-73052df89fda?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)
* * *
* **STATE GOVERNMENT**

**" Gov. Greg Abbott says ICE needs to 'recalibrate' mission after deadly Minnesota shooting"** via [Dallas Morning News](https://substack.com/redirect/6e00b172-645e-4a7d-9d6b-851cb6b59231?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) - Gov. **Greg Abbott** said Monday the White House needs to "recalibrate" Immigration and Customs Enforcement's mission to rebuild public trust after federal agents fatally shot a second protester in Minnesota.

Abbott said immigration authorities should "get back to what they wanted to do to begin with" and focus on removing "people from the country who are here illegally."

He said respect for law enforcement, including ICE, is essential and that the Trump administration needs "to recalibrate on what needs to be done" to restore that respect, "especially under the current circumstances."

On conservative radio host **Mark Davis** ' Dallas-based show, Abbott said he believes federal officials are working on a "game plan" for agents to carry out removals "in a more structured way," without causing "all the kinds of problems and fighting in communities" seen now.

(_[DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/e78a7487-6356-4c02-a5ed-761fe1709224?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Abbott Speaks on State Examining H-1B Visas in Schools, Violence in Minnesota"** [The Texan's](https://substack.com/redirect/cb2ca40e-f2ba-43c2-a12e-ba2b61e561e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Mary Elise O 'Bar** - **Greg Abbott** confirmed during a Monday morning radio spot that his office is collecting data on H-1B visas in Texas, particularly in the state's public schools, and also commented on the recent fatal shooting of a man in Minneapolis, Minnesota by a federal immigration agent -- emphasizing Texas' cooperation with law enforcement.

Internal communications published by Quorum Report on Saturday indicated that Abbott was examining H-1B visas in the state's school system via emails sent to leaders of the Texas A&M University System, who were instructed to compile a list of employees on H-1B visas and report back by Monday.

(_[THE TEXAN](https://substack.com/redirect/7fe09c48-4f90-47e2-a505-c95d08a1782b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Abbott widens winter storm disaster to cover 219 Texas counties"** [Midland Reporter-Telegram's](https://substack.com/redirect/ceccbf58-44b1-4608-be85-8c066b633677?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **B. Kay Richter** - AUSTIN -- **Greg Abbott** has amended the state's original disaster declaration for Winter Storm Fern to include 85 additional counties facing impacts from winter weather conditions.

Within the original disaster declaration issued on Thursday, Jan. 22, counties across the Permian Basin, including Midland and Ector, were listed. Additionally, surrounding counties such as Andrews, Borden, Crane, Dawson, Gaines, Glasscock, Howard, Loving, Martin, Reagan, Reeves, Sterling, Upton, Ward and Winkler were part of the original disaster decree. During the weekend, Abbott amended the state's original disaster declaration for the ongoing winter storm to include Pecos County and others.

"Communities across the state have been impacted by the weekend's winter weather," Abbott said in a statement. "I am updating my disaster declaration to include additional counties to help more Texans during these freezing temperatures. Texans should continue to monitor local weather forecasts and road conditions to keep themselves and their families safe."

In all, the disaster declaration includes 219 counties.

(_[MRT](https://substack.com/redirect/ceccbf58-44b1-4608-be85-8c066b633677?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Paxton Confirms Comptroller Can Block ESA Funds for Schools Tied to Terrorist Groups"** [The Texan's](https://substack.com/redirect/ddd70b54-6e0b-44f4-91e3-1e128f6b13d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Meridith Dyer** - Texas Attorney General **Ken Paxton** issued a legal opinion confirming the Texas Comptroller of Public Accounts' full authority to block taxpayer funds from going to schools tied to terrorist groups or foreign adversaries, according to a January 24 press release.

On December 12, **Hancock** requested an opinion from Paxton asking if schools with such ties are legally disqualified from participating in the state's education savings account program, Texas Education Freedom Accounts (TEFA).

(_[THE TEXAN](https://substack.com/redirect/d561beb9-944e-4726-8d22-27c19ae987dd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)
* * *
* **LOCAL GOVERNMENT**

**" Dallas City Council Resolves Not to Support Elevated High-Speed Rail"** [The Texan's](https://substack.com/redirect/708875c3-b30a-4d33-b58c-a6d2a4c54c53?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Kim Roberts** - The Dallas City Council passed a resolution last week that reaffirms its 2024 decision that any high-speed rail line between Dallas and Fort Worth should not be built above-ground through the Central Business District, Victory Park, nor the uptown area of the city.

However, Councilmember **Paul Ridley** noted that the future of the high-speed rail line is now more uncertain, as last week Congress defunded $928 million in high-speed rail grants during its negotiation of a transportation appropriations bill.

(_[THE TEXAN](https://substack.com/redirect/2e096145-7c20-4259-80e7-bcd1cd1aaf5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

The Federal Transit Administration (FTA) has issued its record of decision, finding that the project meets the environmental requirements and approving its environmental impact statement.

* **2026**

**" Top Hunt aide charged taxpayers $44,000 in hotel expenses" **[Politico's](https://substack.com/redirect/85b16d82-3592-4273-ad75-ebf993972435?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**Daniel Lippman** - The travel expenses of a Republican campaign manager in the crowded Texas Senate race have raised eyebrows for their seemingly exorbitant amount.

**James Kyrkanides** , the campaign manager for Senate candidate **Wesley Hunt,** charged taxpayers more than $44,000 in hotel expenses over three years as Hunt's congressional chief of staff. The charges average $457 per night, four times the [national standard rate set by General Services Administration rate](https://substack.com/redirect/17c1b525-9ddd-4282-aa00-f80180be7943?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for many federal employees, according to a POLITICO analysis of House expense records.

That total accrued over 97 nights during 49 different stays. For instance, on Jan. 3, 2025, when Congress was being sworn in, Kyrkanides expensed a one-night hotel bill of $805. For May 20 to 22, 2024, he submitted a hotel bill of $1,692. And for a stay starting Aug. 6, 2024, he expensed two nights for a total of $1,321.

The expense records do not require the hotels or the city in which they're located to be listed, and Kyrkanides declined to name the hotels.

Kyrkanides said all of the expenses were reviewed and approved by the House finance office, which handles disbursements of member money. He said less than half of the money that was reimbursed went to him personally and the remainder covered travel expenses for other Hunt staffers. He declined to provide receipts to verify that claim.

Kyrkanides confirmed Washington was his primary workplace during that period of time. He said a number of the reimbursements were in Hunt's hometown of Houston, where the GSA's [recommended federal lodging rate](https://substack.com/redirect/86bf8aab-98a8-4b2d-9222-9ad1fb824653?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is $128 per night. Krykanides said expenses also included stays in Washington, San Diego and New York, which have had average GSA rates of $234, $205 and $277 over the last year, respectively. Krykanides said the hotel stays in those cities were expensed on behalf of Hunt staffers for work travel, including a district director conference in San Diego and communications staffers going to New York for Fox News interviews related to policy.

Kyrkanides also noted that costs of food, beverage and parking aren't included in the GSA rates, but his reimbursements folded in those items. He said subtracting them brings his average nightly lodging costs, excluding taxes, to $269. He also pointed to GSA policy that allows federal employees to be reimbursed up to 300 percent of the recommended rate if [no other rooms](https://substack.com/redirect/384b9599-8e74-4f86-a254-e199f8bfb9b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are available.

Asked for comment about the expenses, a Hunt spokesperson said in a statement: "All transactions in this office are approved by the chief administrative officer, made public, and carried out in accordance with the highest ethical guidelines."" [Politico](https://substack.com/redirect/85b16d82-3592-4273-ad75-ebf993972435?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**" Sen. Ted Cruz pitches 2026 Texas Senate race while steering most donations to his campaign" **Dallas Morning News'**Joseph Morton** - It starts as an [urgent warning](https://substack.com/redirect/764e74cb-1230-4b7d-ae1c-ff0db701faa7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

Texas' primary is speeding closer, Sen. **Ted Cruz** says, the nation is watching and Republicans need to get off the sidelines to win in 2026.

Later come buttons to make a donation, a familiar step in fundraising emails. The pitch reassures your money will help both Cruz and the future Republican nominee for the U.S. Senate in Texas, a two-for-one play to counter Democrats.

If you stop to click on a small-type link for additional details, the math appears. With a $100 donation, $1 is dedicated to a new fund for whoever wins the GOP nomination.

The rest, $99, goes to Cruz's campaign account, unless donors change the allocation.

Cruz's recent appeal to conservative donors is a staple of modern campaign fundraising emails, with preset donation amounts, flashing buttons and fine print dividing contributions.

The approach is legal and reflects the growing prevalence of aggressive political email appeals that critics say are often manipulative.

Cruz's effort has stirred questions in Washington, with early donations meant to fully help the nominee largely being steered to Cruz.

Some election law experts called it deceptive, trading on concerns about the Texas Senate race to benefit Cruz, who is not on the 2026 ballot but has been mentioned as a presidential contender in two years.

Attention over campaign money is even sharper this cycle because of the high-profile, [high-spending Republican primary](https://substack.com/redirect/f435549d-2d62-486e-ab8c-e5431d19db21?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) among Sen.**John Cornyn** , Attorney General **Ken Paxton** and U.S. Rep. **Wesley Hunt** of Houston.

"Cruz is basically diverting money away from the Cornyn-Paxton race for his own personal ambition," said campaign finance lawyer **Brett Kappel** , a former legal counsel to three presidential campaigns.

Cruz spokesperson **Macarena Martinez** dismissed that, saying his outreach is aimed at keeping Texas Republican and ensuring a GOP victory in November.

"No Republican in the country has devoted more time and spent more money to elect Republican senators and House members than Sen. Cruz," she said.

She said any criticism of Cruz's appeal "conveniently ignores the well-known fact that this standard split is routinely used by both Democrats and Republicans when a campaign pays the cost of a digital solicitation."

The Cornyn, Paxton and Hunt campaigns declined to comment on Cruz's fundraising approach."

(_[DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/2f68944f-fed2-4706-a9e5-56790b39e5ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Heated GOP Primary for Southeast Texas House District Draws Scrutiny Over Public School Education Foundation Spending"** [The Texan's](https://substack.com/redirect/afc97270-bc42-4e55-bd7f-a56afc6b5c11?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Holly Hansen** - A heated Republican battle is emerging in southeast Texas, where state Rep. **Terri Leo Wilson** (R-Galveston) faces a primary challenge from a Chambers County developer in a race shaped by conflicting characterizations of Leo Wilson's voting record -- and renewed scrutiny of a tiny school district's relationship with a lucrative non-profit public-school foundation.

Former Mont Belvieu City Manager **Nathan Watkins** , vice president at Americus Holdings Real Estate Development and a director of the Barbers Hill Independent School District's (BHISD) education foundation, announced last year that he would run against Leo Wilson for Texas House District 23, which includes Chambers County and part of Galveston County.

(_[THE TEXAN](https://substack.com/redirect/7193955d-40d8-4dc7-b610-ef6b42fb6720?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

* **TEXANS IN DC**

**" Rep. Julie Johnson traded stocks from company that helps Trump's deportation efforts despite anti-ICE comments"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/9885e203-bf28-43e9-972c-9a14b586972e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Olivia Borgula** - **Julie Johnson** , a Farmers Branch Democrat who was elected in 2024 to represent Texas' 32rd Congressional District, bought and sold stocks from Palantir Technologies, a contractor with the federal government that has been key to the Trump administration's efforts to track and deport undocumented immigrants.

Johnson bought Palantir stock days before President**Donald Trump** took office for his second term on Jan. 15, 2025, and again on Feb. 12, 2025, according to congressional financial disclosure reports. She sold her shares a few months later in April and June of 2025, the reports show, and for each sale she reported earning between $1,001 and $15,000. In a statement to the Texas Tribune, Johnson said the stocks were managed through independent third parties and that she started divesting her holdings in March, and that all Palantir holdings were sold by June.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/21957526-7960-4d81-9d83-f70fa4b16413?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

Thank you for reading as a FREE subscriber. A WORD FROM SENATOR PHIL GRAMM ABOUT MUST READ TEXAS " If something of importance is known in Texas, Matt knows it. With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed." - Former U.S. Senator Phil Gramm (R-TX) AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to , a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin? | | ---|---|--- Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here: . Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . TOP NEWS " Texas stunner: Democrat Taylor Rehmet flips Republican state Senate district Trump won by 17 points" AP's John Hanna andJulie Carr Smyth - DemocratTaylor Rehmet flipped a reliably Republican state Senate district in Texas in Saturday's special election, continuing a string of surprise victories for Democrats across the U.S. in the year sinceDonald Trump returned to the White House. The Republican president immediately distanced himself from the loss. It's a district he had won by 17 points in 2024. At Mar-a-Lago on Sunday, Trump told reporters, "I'm not involved in that. That's a local Texas race." Just a day earlier, however, he had used his social media platform to urge voters to support the Republican candidate, conservative activist Leigh Wambsganns. He called her a successful entrepreneur and "an incredible supporter" of his Make America Great Again movement. Rehmet, a labor union leader and veteran, defeated Wambsganss easily in the Fort Worth-area district. With almost all votes counted, Rehmet was leading by more than 14 percentage points -- a more than 30-point difference. "This win goes to everyday working people," Rehmet told supporters. Republican Texas Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick called it "a wake-up call for Republicans across Texas," where the GOP controls every statewide office. "Our voters cannot take anything for granted," Patrick wrote on the social platform X, while noting low-turnout special elections are always unpredictable. "I know the energy and strength the Republican grassroots in Texas possess. We will come out fighting with a new resolve, and we will take this seat back in November." Rehmet's victory added to Democrats' record of overperforming in special elections so far this cycle, beginning in March -- when they prevailed in a Pennsylvania legislative district made up of suburbanites and farmers that -- and continuing through to November, when they from Maine to California. ... Democrats said Saturday's results in Texas were further evidence that voters under the second Trump administration are motivated to reject GOP candidates and their policies. Texas Democratic Party Chair Kendall Scudder said Rehmet won by standing with working people and talking to Texans about the future. "This win shows what is possible in Texas with strong organizing, great candidates and strategic investments," he said in a statement. "People are noticing that Democrats have the workers' backs and are delivering results."" " Deadly, drug-resistant fungus spreading in Texas. Here's who is at risk" via - A deadly, drug-resistant fungus has infected more than 27 states in the last year, including Texas. It's called Candida auris. Since its emergence in the U.S. in 2016, the fungus has had a 34 percent mortality rate between 2016 and 2022, according to data from the Center for Disease Control, which now considers C. auris as a notifiable infectious disease. As of Dec. 27, 2025 Texas reported 830 clinical cases of the fungal infection for that year. Here's what to know about Candida auris in Texas. Candida auris is a yeast infection common in sick patients at healthcare facilities. C. auris can cause both skin or bloodstream infections. The fungus commonly enters the body through pathways via medical devices like catheters, breathing or feeding tubes. What makes it drug resistant is its ability to resist antifungal medications. "The fungus develops the ability to defeat the drugs designed to kill it," according to the CDC. "That means the germs are not killed and continue to grow." () STATE GOVERNMENT " Gov. Greg Abbott says ICE needs to 'recalibrate' mission after deadly Minnesota shooting" via - Gov. Greg Abbott said Monday the White House needs to "recalibrate" Immigration and Customs Enforcement's mission to rebuild public trust after federal agents fatally shot a second protester in Minnesota. Abbott said immigration authorities should "get back to what they wanted to do to begin with" and focus on removing "people from the country who are here illegally." He said respect for law enforcement, including ICE, is essential and that the Trump administration needs "to recalibrate on what needs to be done" to restore that respect, "especially under the current circumstances." On conservative radio host Mark Davis ' Dallas-based show, Abbott said he believes federal officials are working on a "game plan" for agents to carry out removals "in a more structured way," without causing "all the kinds of problems and fighting in communities" seen now. () " Abbott Speaks on State Examining H-1B Visas in Schools, Violence in Minnesota" Mary Elise O 'Bar - Greg Abbott confirmed during a Monday morning radio spot that his office is collecting data on H-1B visas in Texas, particularly in the state's public schools, and also commented on the recent fatal shooting of a man in Minneapolis, Minnesota by a federal immigration agent -- emphasizing Texas' cooperation with law enforcement. Internal communications published by Quorum Report on Saturday indicated that Abbott was examining H-1B visas in the state's school system via emails sent to leaders of the Texas A&M University System, who were instructed to compile a list of employees on H-1B visas and report back by Monday. () " Abbott widens winter storm disaster to cover 219 Texas counties" B. Kay Richter - AUSTIN -- Greg Abbott has amended the state's original disaster declaration for Winter Storm Fern to include 85 additional counties facing impacts from winter weather conditions. Within the original disaster declaration issued on Thursday, Jan. 22, counties across the Permian Basin, including Midland and Ector, were listed. Additionally, surrounding counties such as Andrews, Borden, Crane, Dawson, Gaines, Glasscock, Howard, Loving, Martin, Reagan, Reeves, Sterling, Upton, Ward and Winkler were part of the original disaster decree. During the weekend, Abbott amended the state's original disaster declaration for the ongoing winter storm to include Pecos County and others. "Communities across the state have been impacted by the weekend's winter weather," Abbott said in a statement. "I am updating my disaster declaration to include additional counties to help more Texans during these freezing temperatures. Texans should continue to monitor local weather forecasts and road conditions to keep themselves and their families safe." In all, the disaster declaration includes 219 counties. () " Paxton Confirms Comptroller Can Block ESA Funds for Schools Tied to Terrorist Groups" Meridith Dyer - Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton issued a legal opinion confirming the Texas Comptroller of Public Accounts' full authority to block taxpayer funds from going to schools tied to terrorist groups or foreign adversaries, according to a January 24 press release. On December 12, Hancock requested an opinion from Paxton asking if schools with such ties are legally disqualified from participating in the state's education savings account program, Texas Education Freedom Accounts (TEFA). () LOCAL GOVERNMENT " Dallas City Council Resolves Not to Support Elevated High-Speed Rail" Kim Roberts - The Dallas City Council passed a resolution last week that reaffirms its 2024 decision that any high-speed rail line between Dallas and Fort Worth should not be built above-ground through the Central Business District, Victory Park, nor the uptown area of the city. However, Councilmember Paul Ridley noted that the future of the high-speed rail line is now more uncertain, as last week Congress defunded $928 million in high-speed rail grants during its negotiation of a transportation appropriations bill. () " Austin Light Rail Proposal Passes Environmental Assessment in Effort for Federal Funding" Kim Roberts - The Austin Transit Partnership is celebrating that its light rail system has completed a "major milestone" required before construction can begin. The Federal Transit Administration (FTA) has issued its record of decision, finding that the project meets the environmental requirements and approving its environmental impact statement. () 2026 " Top Hunt aide charged taxpayers $44,000 in hotel expenses" Lippman - The travel expenses of a Republican campaign manager in the crowded Texas Senate race have raised eyebrows for their seemingly exorbitant amount. James Kyrkanides , the campaign manager for Senate candidate Wesley Hunt, charged taxpayers more than $44,000 in hotel expenses over three years as Hunt's congressional chief of staff. The charges average $457 per night, four times the for many federal employees, according to a POLITICO analysis of House expense records. That total accrued over 97 nights during 49 different stays. For instance, on Jan. 3, 2025, when Congress was being sworn in, Kyrkanides expensed a one-night hotel bill of $805. For May 20 to 22, 2024, he submitted a hotel bill of $1,692. And for a stay starting Aug. 6, 2024, he expensed two nights for a total of $1,321. The expense records do not require the hotels or the city in which they're located to be listed, and Kyrkanides declined to name the hotels. Kyrkanides said all of the expenses were reviewed and approved by the House finance office, which handles disbursements of member money. He said less than half of the money that was reimbursed went to him personally and the remainder covered travel expenses for other Hunt staffers. He declined to provide receipts to verify that claim. Kyrkanides confirmed Washington was his primary workplace during that period of time. He said a number of the reimbursements were in Hunt's hometown of Houston, where the GSA's is $128 per night. Krykanides said expenses also included stays in Washington, San Diego and New York, which have had average GSA rates of $234, $205 and $277 over the last year, respectively. Krykanides said the hotel stays in those cities were expensed on behalf of Hunt staffers for work travel, including a district director conference in San Diego and communications staffers going to New York for Fox News interviews related to policy. Kyrkanides also noted that costs of food, beverage and parking aren't included in the GSA rates, but his reimbursements folded in those items. He said subtracting them brings his average nightly lodging costs, excluding taxes, to $269. He also pointed to GSA policy that allows federal employees to be reimbursed up to 300 percent of the recommended rate if are available. Asked for comment about the expenses, a Hunt spokesperson said in a statement: "All transactions in this office are approved by the chief administrative officer, made public, and carried out in accordance with the highest ethical guidelines."" " Sen. Ted Cruz pitches 2026 Texas Senate race while steering most donations to his campaign" Dallas Morning News'Joseph Morton - It starts as an . Texas' primary is speeding closer, Sen. Ted Cruz says, the nation is watching and Republicans need to get off the sidelines to win in 2026. Later come buttons to make a donation, a familiar step in fundraising emails. The pitch reassures your money will help both Cruz and the future Republican nominee for the U.S. Senate in Texas, a two-for-one play to counter Democrats. If you stop to click on a small-type link for additional details, the math appears. With a $100 donation, $1 is dedicated to a new fund for whoever wins the GOP nomination. The rest, $99, goes to Cruz's campaign account, unless donors change the allocation. Cruz's recent appeal to conservative donors is a staple of modern campaign fundraising emails, with preset donation amounts, flashing buttons and fine print dividing contributions. The approach is legal and reflects the growing prevalence of aggressive political email appeals that critics say are often manipulative. Cruz's effort has stirred questions in Washington, with early donations meant to fully help the nominee largely being steered to Cruz. Some election law experts called it deceptive, trading on concerns about the Texas Senate race to benefit Cruz, who is not on the 2026 ballot but has been mentioned as a presidential contender in two years. Attention over campaign money is even sharper this cycle because of the high-profile, among Sen.John Cornyn , Attorney General Ken Paxton and U.S. Rep. Wesley Hunt of Houston. "Cruz is basically diverting money away from the Cornyn-Paxton race for his own personal ambition," said campaign finance lawyer Brett Kappel , a former legal counsel to three presidential campaigns. Cruz spokesperson Macarena Martinez dismissed that, saying his outreach is aimed at keeping Texas Republican and ensuring a GOP victory in November. "No Republican in the country has devoted more time and spent more money to elect Republican senators and House members than Sen. Cruz," she said. She said any criticism of Cruz's appeal "conveniently ignores the well-known fact that this standard split is routinely used by both Democrats and Republicans when a campaign pays the cost of a digital solicitation." The Cornyn, Paxton and Hunt campaigns declined to comment on Cruz's fundraising approach." () " Heated GOP Primary for Southeast Texas House District Draws Scrutiny Over Public School Education Foundation Spending" Holly Hansen - A heated Republican battle is emerging in southeast Texas, where state Rep. Terri Leo Wilson (R-Galveston) faces a primary challenge from a Chambers County developer in a race shaped by conflicting characterizations of Leo Wilson's voting record -- and renewed scrutiny of a tiny school district's relationship with a lucrative non-profit public-school foundation. Former Mont Belvieu City Manager Nathan Watkins , vice president at Americus Holdings Real Estate Development and a director of the Barbers Hill Independent School District's (BHISD) education foundation, announced last year that he would run against Leo Wilson for Texas House District 23, which includes Chambers County and part of Galveston County. () TEXANS IN DC " Rep. Julie Johnson traded stocks from company that helps Trump's deportation efforts despite anti-ICE comments" Olivia Borgula - Julie Johnson , a Farmers Branch Democrat who was elected in 2024 to represent Texas' 32rd Congressional District, bought and sold stocks from Palantir Technologies, a contractor with the federal government that has been key to the Trump administration's efforts to track and deport undocumented immigrants. Johnson bought Palantir stock days before PresidentDonald Trump took office for his second term on Jan. 15, 2025, and again on Feb. 12, 2025, according to congressional financial disclosure reports. She sold her shares a few months later in April and June of 2025, the reports show, and for each sale she reported earning between $1,001 and $15,000. In a statement to the Texas Tribune, Johnson said the stocks were managed through independent third parties and that she started divesting her holdings in March, and that all Palantir holdings were sold by June. * A candidate caught redhanded being funded directly by AIPAC donors with a history of giving to Trump and attended a fundraiser hosted by the AIPAC board president himself. She continues to lie to Democrats about their support.

This should alarm Democrats everywhere. AIPAC is bankrolled by the same billionaire donors who helped elect Trump, and now they are trying to influence the outcome of an election in one of the bluest congressional seats in the entire country.
I was born and raised in IL-9. I have a track record of showing up for my community, telling the truth, and fighting to better the lives of my neighbors. We have built this campaign from the support of my neighbors and with the support of thousands of Democrats from across the nation.
But to win, it will require taking on the endless pockets of the MAGA billionaires. And they have a clown car full of candidates ready to sell their soul for this seat. It's going to take everything we have to stop them.
With you on my side, we will defeat AIPAC:

clean_body: AIPAC is coalescing to buy this open seat and they are turning this primary into a circus. | | | | | == | Peter, News just broke that another of my opponents in this race sought the support of AIPAC, the PAC MAGA uses to launder far-right donor funds into Democratic primaries. My opponent still refuses to call what is happening in Gaza a genocide and has accepted the endorsement of 314 Action, a group that was exposed in 2024 for funneling AIPAC money into a Democratic primary race in Oregon. | | == | In the IL-9 Democratic primary, we have: A candidate who begged for AIPAC's support and later was endorsed by 314 Action, which has a track record of colluding with AIPAC to secretly funnel money into dem primaries. A candidate caught redhanded being funded directly by AIPAC donors with a history of giving to Trump and attended a fundraiser hosted by the AIPAC board president himself. She continues to lie to Democrats about their support. * A candidate who suspended his campaign after AIPAC pressured him behind the scenes to drop out, like a good little boy. | | == | AIPAC is coalescing to buy this open seat and they are turning this primary into a circus. This should alarm Democrats everywhere. AIPAC is bankrolled by the same billionaire donors who helped elect Trump, and now they are trying to influence the outcome of an election in one of the bluest congressional seats in the entire country. I was born and raised in IL-9. I have a track record of showing up for my community, telling the truth, and fighting to better the lives of my neighbors. We have built this campaign from the support of my neighbors and with the support of thousands of Democrats from across the nation. But to win, it will require taking on the endless pockets of the MAGA billionaires. And they have a clown car full of candidates ready to sell their soul for this seat. It's going to take everything we have to stop them. “If I hear bad news about the stock market one more time, I’m gonna be sick.”
We get it. Investors are rattled, costs keep rising, and the world keeps getting weirder.
So, who’s better at handling their money than the uber-rich?
Have 3 long-term investing tips UBS (Swiss bank) shared for shaky times:
1. Hold extra cash for expenses and buying cheap if markets fall.
2. Diversify outside stocks (Gold, real estate, etc.).
3. Hold a slice of wealth in alternatives that tend not to move with equities.

The catch? Most alternatives aren’t open to everyday investors
That’s why [Masterworks](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/v1/c/sLYlFrEGAW0%2Bi7RsSOaLkvmrFyvTGVxeeMnPAipNYasko97kRUSAPz0EXjms%0AWMaMGLXicFlyBLz6hV4orelo3PIsV2imEzVWwjfIGJltdnyHvSQwmZiCe5f6%0A70IMckJPSKroarrPB5flX%2Bs8zmlbVYlsm19IBZafYPvlsq%2FWWokVFt02IKY%2F%0AfOsAXmbYQYZw48L8E%2F%2Fho8KrwnsODf7MT6ly3PBHzVnNH7YYNkSMgXijLrgl%0A2f8ywtkrY6Mt2p761wVp1hswaNVHPYwV9sS0iY8cnegyuaKtqIB%2BgDlJnwIm%0ALaJ5Du6awLcWfeBQ5v3IyOtf7wrnOZCAgdhSS6LaHuB%2BPfp6JohUVdkkNmSs%0Au5U%3D%0A/f6ffd638aa1ba4b9) exists: 70,000+ members invest in shares of something that’s appreciated more overall than the S&P; 500 over 30 years without moving in lockstep with it.*
